basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Ṭā Hā — the sūra of tenderness — opens with a word of relief addressed to the Prophet: “We have not sent down the Qurʾān upon you that you be distressed.” The Name that governs it is al-Raḥmān, the All-Merciful established upon the Throne, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens, on the earth, between them, and beneath the moist soil. Then unfolds the long story of Moses: the fire glimpsed in the night, the call by name, the sandals removed in the holy valley, the staff turned into a serpent and the hand made white, the mission to Pharaoh with the overwhelming command to “speak to him a gentle word,” the contest of the magicians who, defeated, prostrate and believe — in the morning sorcerers, in the evening chosen ones —, the crossing of the sea, then the golden calf of al-Sāmirī and the return of Moses, angry and grieved. At last comes Adam, who forgot the covenant, was tempted by the desire for immortality, disobeyed, then was chosen anew: Qushayrī observes that He who had first chosen him with no cause chose him again after the fall — the second election is sweeter than the first. The sūra culminates on the highest prayer: “say: My Lord, increase me in knowledge” (rabbi zidnī ʿilman) — for if this is said to the most learned of men, it is because the subtleties with which God favors His close ones are without limit. It is sealed on patience, glorification at both ends of the day, and the waiting in which the believers repose in the breath of tawḥīd.

versets.ar: طٰه
versets.tr: ṭā hā
versets.trad: Ṭā Hā.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads these two letters as a secret of orientation. The ṭāʾ points to the Prophet's heart, turned away from all that is not God; the hāʾ, that same heart guided toward Him alone. One also hears: “tread the carpet of nearness, for you will find your way to none but Us”; We have folded away from your secret the remembrance of all that is not Us. Blessed, and sweet is the life of whoever lets himself be guided by you.

mots.racine: ط ه
mots.def: opening letters; read as an address: “O man!”.

versets.ar: مَآ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ لِتَشْقَىٰٓ
versets.tr: mā anzalnā ʿalayka l-qurʾāna li-tashqā
versets.trad: We have not sent down upon you the Qurʾān that you be distressed,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Revelation was not laid upon you for your distress; it is but the opening of union, the spreading of the carpet of nearness. When he was told not to lift his eyes toward the world's enjoyments, the beloved stood upon the tip of one foot, out of modesty to approach it; then it was said to him: “set both your feet upon the earth” — why all this toil? — and he answered: “shall I not be a thankful servant?”, overwhelmed with gratitude for the grace that made him able to worship.

mots.racine: ن ز ل
ش ق و
mots.def: shaqā: to be wretched, to toil.

versets.ar: إِلَّا تَذْكِرَةً لِّمَن يَخْشَىٰ
versets.tr: illā tadhkiratan li-man yakhshā
versets.trad: but only as a Reminder for whoever fears God,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Qurʾān is clear sight for the people of reason, remembrance for the people of arrival. The first are illumined by it and find in it the soul's repose for their hereafter; the second remember through it and taste here and now the breath of intimacy (uns). The Word fructifies only the heart already made tender by reverent awe.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر
خ ش ي

versets.ar: تَنزِيلًا مِّمَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ ٱلْعُلَى
versets.tr: tanzīlan mimman khalaqa l-arḍa wa-s-samāwāti l-ʿulā
versets.trad: a revelation from the One who created the earth and the high heavens,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Word descends from Him who created the earth as a stable abode. And just as the earth is rest for bodies, the souls of the worshippers are an earth and a dwelling for their obedience, and the hearts of the knowers a dwelling for their gnosis. The majesty of the Source guarantees the grandeur of what it lays down in the lowest place.

mots.racine: ن ز ل
mots.def: tanzīl: the gradual descent of the Word.

mots.racine: س م و / ع ل و

versets.ar: ٱلرَّحْمَٰنُ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ٱسْتَوَىٰ
versets.tr: ar-raḥmānu ʿalā l-ʿarshi stawā
versets.trad: the All-Merciful established upon the Throne.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ventures here a famous parallel: the All-Merciful's establishment (istiwāʾ) upon the Throne of heaven is taken for granted; but His throne upon earth is the hearts of the people of tawḥīd. Upon the Throne of heaven He is established; upon the throne of hearts He reigns (istawlā). The Throne of heaven is the qibla of creatures' invocations; the throne of the heart, the place where the Real's gaze alights. What a distance between one throne and another!

mots.racine: ر ح م
ع ر ش / س و ي
mots.def: istawā: to settle, to be established.

versets.ar: لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا وَمَا تَحْتَ ٱلثَّرَىٰ
versets.tr: lahu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi wa-mā baynahumā wa-mā taḥta th-tharā
versets.trad: To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth, whatever is between them, and whatever is beneath the soil.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: all things belong to Him in general, by right of ownership; but the privileged friends (awliyāʾ) belong to Him in particular, by election and honor. Whatever was drawn from nothingness into being is wholly His by establishment and creation. Nothing escapes His mastery — not in the heavens, nor on the earth, nor beneath the moist soil.

mots.racine: س م و
mots.sens: to Him what is in the heavens

mots.racine: ث ر ي
mots.def: tharā: moist earth of the depths.

versets.ar: وَإِن تَجْهَرْ بِٱلْقَوْلِ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَعْلَمُ ٱلسِّرَّ وَأَخْفَى
versets.tr: wa-in tajhar bi-l-qawli fa-innahu yaʿlamu s-sirra wa-akhfā
versets.trad: Whether you speak aloud [or not], He surely knows the secret and what is yet more hidden.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the degrees of the hidden: the soul does not reach what is in the heart, the heart does not reach the secrets of the spirit, and the spirit has no path to the realities of the innermost secret. That which is “more hidden than the secret” none knows but the Real: Satan does not corrupt it, the two angels do not record it, and no stranger comes upon it. Whether or not you raise your voice, He knows the unseen and what is yet more hidden.

mots.racine: ج ه ر
س ر ر / خ ف ي
mots.sens: the secret and the more hidden

versets.ar: ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ لَهُ ٱلْأَسْمَآءُ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ
versets.tr: allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa lahu l-asmāʾu l-ḥusnā
versets.trad: God! There is no god but He. To Him belong the most beautiful Names.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has denied to every contingent being that it could bring anything into being, and affirmed that all that exists returns to Him by the right of pre-eternity. As for the most beautiful Names, they are His attributes, and to mention them teaches the creatures that loftiness and transcendence above every imperfection belong to Him alone, in the singularity of His being.

mots.racine: أ ل ه
س م و / ح س ن

versets.ar: وَهَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسَىٰٓ
versets.tr: wa-hal atāka ḥadīthu mūsā
versets.trad: Has the story of Moses reached you?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the divine custom: God mentions the story of Moses wherever He recalls the history of our Prophet. And the question “has the story of Moses reached you?” has the form of inquiry but the value of affirmation. The forerunner's tale comes to console and confirm the beloved: what the chosen servants passed through, you too will pass through under the gaze of Providence.

mots.racine: أ ت ي
ح د ث

versets.ar: إِذْ رَءَا نَارًا فَقَالَ لِأَهْلِهِ ٱمْكُثُوٓا۟ إِنِّىٓ ءَانَسْتُ نَارًا لَّعَلِّىٓ ءَاتِيكُم مِّنْهَا بِقَبَسٍ أَوْ أَجِدُ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ هُدًى
versets.tr: idh raʾā nāran fa-qāla li-ahlihi mkuthū innī ānastu nāran laʿallī ātīkum minhā bi-qabasin aw ajidu ʿalā n-nāri hudan
versets.trad: When he saw a fire, he said to his family: “Stay here! I have glimpsed a fire; perhaps I will bring you a brand from it, or find at the fire some guidance.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the fire was shown to him to draw him out from his people and pull him toward Him; the aim was not the fire but the departure. Moses drew near, and the fire withdrew. O Moses, this fire gives light but grants no one an ember; this fire burns hearts, not bodies. While he sought how to take a spark from it, suddenly the Call of the Real rang out.

mots.racine: أ ن س / ن و ر
mots.def: ānasa: to perceive, to sense with intimacy.

mots.racine: ق ب س
ه د ي

versets.ar: فَلَمَّآ أَتَىٰهَا نُودِىَ يَٰمُوسَىٰٓ
versets.tr: fa-lammā atāhā nūdiya yā mūsā
versets.trad: When he came to it, he was called: “O Moses!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses knew it was the Word of the Real the moment he perceived in it order, arrangement, and composition; some say he knew it by an inner instruction God reserved for him, apart from all reasoning. When he reached the fire he was called by his name: the intimacy of the Call came before any trial.

mots.racine: أ ت ي
ن د و

versets.ar: إِنِّىٓ أَنَا۠ رَبُّكَ فَٱخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ ۖ إِنَّكَ بِٱلْوَادِ ٱلْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى
versets.tr: innī anā rabbuka fa-khlaʿ naʿlayka innaka bi-l-wādi l-muqaddasi ṭuwan
versets.trad: I am, truly, your Lord! Take off your sandals: you are in the holy valley of Ṭuwā.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “remove your two sandals,” for the carpet of the kings' presence is not trodden shod. The allusion runs deeper still: empty your heart of the speech of the two abodes and strip yourself bare for the Real in the attribute of solitude; detach yourself from the two kinds of your acts, efface from your sight the genera of your states — nearness and distance, union and separation, annihilation and subsistence — and be by Our attribute, for you are only by Our right.

mots.racine: خ ل ع / ن ع ل
mots.def: khalaʿa: to remove, to strip away.

mots.racine: و د ي / ق د س
mots.def: muqaddas: sanctified; Ṭuwā: name of the place.

versets.ar: وَأَنَا ٱخْتَرْتُكَ فَٱسْتَمِعْ لِمَا يُوحَىٰٓ
versets.tr: wa-anā khtartuka fa-stamiʿ li-mā yūḥā
versets.trad: I have chosen you. So listen to what is revealed:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “I have chosen you in full knowledge of you; I have stripped and purified you of the defilement of illusions and all that clouds your limpidity.” And it is said: once I have chosen you, you are Mine and by Me, effaced in your extinction to yourself. Listen, then, to what is revealed, for whoever the Real has elected listens to none but Him.

mots.racine: خ ي ر
س م ع / و ح ي

versets.ar: إِنَّنِىٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعْبُدْنِى وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِذِكْرِىٓ
versets.tr: innanī anā llāhu lā ilāha illā anā fa-ʿbudnī wa-aqimi ṣ-ṣalāta li-dhikrī
versets.trad: Truly, I am God! There is no god but Me. So worship Me, and perform the prayer for My remembrance.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “there is no god but Me” — the others-than-Him are sheer absence in My existence, and traces and ruins are effaced once My right is established. “Worship Me”: bow to My decree, carry out My command, bend before the majesty of My sovereignty. As for the prayer “performed for My remembrance,” whoever sets it up without regard to the One who makes it run falls into self-conceit; but whoever establishes it while witnessing that Another sets it in motion makes of it the opening of union and the place of intimate discourse.

mots.racine: ع ب د
ق و م / ص ل و
ذ ك ر

versets.ar: إِنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ ءَاتِيَةٌ أَكَادُ أُخْفِيهَا لِتُجْزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍۭ بِمَا تَسْعَىٰ
versets.tr: inna s-sāʿata ātiyatun akādu ukhfīhā li-tujzā kullu nafsin bi-mā tasʿā
versets.trad: The Hour is surely coming! I keep it almost hidden, that every soul be requited for what it strives after.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the use of warning the servants of the Hour's nearness is that they awaken from the heedlessness of dispersion. And when they are present in heart, in the permanence of remembrance, what is promised for the future is already given them in the instant: their present is like their hereafter. So the witnessing of the moment as resurrection was made a sign of uprightness.

mots.racine: س و ع / أ ت ي
خ ف ي

versets.ar: فَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكَ عَنْهَا مَن لَّا يُؤْمِنُ بِهَا وَٱتَّبَعَ هَوَىٰهُ فَتَرْدَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-lā yaṣuddannaka ʿanhā man lā yuʾminu bihā wa-ttabaʿa hawāhu fa-tardā
versets.trad: So let not the one who does not believe in it and follows his passion turn you away from it, lest you perish.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when God honors the servant with a fair awakening and makes him present in the attribute of witnessing, it does not befit him to descend from the heaven of his attributes into the hell of the heedless, nor to fall back into the valleys of dispersion where those who deny the Hour and follow their whim go astray.

mots.racine: ص د د
mots.sens: let him not turn you away

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ه و ي
mots.def: hawā: desire, caprice of the soul.

versets.ar: وَمَا تِلْكَ بِيَمِينِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-mā tilka bi-yamīnika yā mūsā
versets.trad: And what is that in your right hand, O Moses?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the question about the staff was repeated to him because the Real had deposited in it the great wonder; and also, because the majesty of the encounter had seized him at the sudden irruption of the Word, he was brought back to hearing the story of the staff to settle some of the awe overwhelming him. Had he been left under the weight of dread, perhaps he would have grasped nothing more.

mots.racine: ي م ن
mots.sens: what is that in your right hand

versets.ar: قَالَ هِىَ عَصَاىَ أَتَوَكَّؤُا۟ عَلَيْهَا وَأَهُشُّ بِهَا عَلَىٰ غَنَمِى وَلِىَ فِيهَا مَـَٔارِبُ أُخْرَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla hiya ʿaṣāya atawakkaʾu ʿalayhā wa-ahushshu bihā ʿalā ghanamī wa-liya fīhā maʾāribu ukhrā
versets.trad: He replied: “It is my staff, on which I lean and with which I beat down leaves for my sheep; and I have other uses for it.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: faced with the call to throw down the staff, Moses began to list its uses, and the Real showed him the secret: you stand, by the attribute of tawḥīd, upon the carpet of solitude — how then could it befit you to have a prop on which you lean, a support in which you seek help? The first step on the Path is the abandonment of every cause; and the more Moses detailed his benefit, the worthier he became of believing by casting it away and of purifying himself of every support other than God.

mots.racine: ع ص و
و ك أ
أ ر ب

versets.ar: قَالَ أَلْقِهَا يَٰمُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla alqihā yā mūsā
versets.trad: He said: “Cast it down, O Moses!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: tawḥīd is stripping bare (tajrīd), and the sign of its soundness is the fall of all self-attribution. So, the moment Moses named his uses of the staff, he was commanded to throw it down. “Cast it, O Moses”: let fall what you ascribe help to, that no support may remain for you apart from Him. The command to cast is the command to abandon every means.

mots.racine: ل ق ي

versets.ar: فَأَلْقَىٰهَا فَإِذَا هِىَ حَيَّةٌ تَسْعَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-alqāhā fa-idhā hiya ḥayyatun tasʿā
versets.trad: He cast it down, and behold, it became a serpent gliding.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God made it a serpent that crawled, and Moses fled without turning back; it was said to him: “behold the image of attachment: when its secret is unveiled to the one who carries it, he flees from it.” What seemed a support proved a menace, for no one should trust the appearance of things: the staff became a serpent, and the outward deceives before its opposite is unveiled.

mots.racine: ل ق ي
ح ي ي / س ع ي
mots.def: ḥayya: serpent; saʿā: to move swiftly.

versets.ar: قَالَ خُذْهَا وَلَا تَخَفْ ۖ سَنُعِيدُهَا سِيرَتَهَا ٱلْأُولَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla khudhhā wa-lā takhaf sa-nuʿīduhā sīratahā l-ūlā
versets.trad: He said: “Take hold of it and do not fear! We shall restore it to its former state.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “take it and do not fear” — through the staff's turning, now staff, now serpent, then staff again, God showed that He steadies His servants in the state of variation (talwīn): He takes and gives back, He gathers and parts, until they reach stability. The aim was not an ordeal but a sign and a wonder: that they might know that the secrets of the decrees none knows but the All-Powerful.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / خ و ف
mots.def: khadha: to take; khāfa: to fear.

mots.racine: س ي ر / أ و ل

versets.ar: وَٱضْمُمْ يَدَكَ إِلَىٰ جَنَاحِكَ تَخْرُجْ بَيْضَآءَ مِنْ غَيْرِ سُوٓءٍ ءَايَةً أُخْرَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-ḍmum yadaka ilā janāḥika takhruj bayḍāʾa min ghayri sūʾin āyatan ukhrā
versets.trad: And press your hand to your side; it will come out white, unblemished by any ill. That is another sign,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: as He showed him a sign without, He showed him one within himself — the hand drawn out white, with no harm. This is the sense of: “We shall show them Our signs upon the horizons and within themselves.” The inner sign takes precedence in dignity over the outer, for what a man finds in his own soul is the nearest proof.

mots.racine: ض م م
mots.def: ḍamma: to press, to draw close.

mots.racine: ب ي ض / س و أ

versets.ar: لِنُرِيَكَ مِنْ ءَايَٰتِنَا ٱلْكُبْرَى
versets.tr: li-nuriyaka min āyātinā l-kubrā
versets.trad: that We may show you some of Our greatest signs.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the greatest of signs (al-āya al-kubrā) is neither the staff nor the hand, but what Moses found within himself of witnessing and presence — those states the servant neither produces nor governs, which their possessor grasps only by direct taste (dhawq). The highest wonder takes place within.

mots.racine: ر أ ي
أ ي ي / ك ب ر

versets.ar: ٱذْهَبْ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ
versets.tr: idhhab ilā firʿawna innahu ṭaghā
versets.trad: Go to Pharaoh; truly he has transgressed!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: after making him hear His Word without intermediary, honoring his station and lavishing dignity upon him, He commanded him to go to Pharaoh — knowing he would neither believe nor listen. It was hard for Moses, after hearing the Word of God, to go and face Pharaoh's denial; but he preferred the command of his trial to the desire of his soul.

mots.racine: ذ ه ب
ط غ ي

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱشْرَحْ لِى صَدْرِى
versets.tr: qāla rabbi shraḥ lī ṣadrī
versets.trad: Moses said: “My Lord! Expand my breast for me,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: thus Moses, charged to go to Pharaoh, asked for the provision of the journey and that by which the delivery of the Mission is accomplished. “My Lord, open for me my breast” — that I may endure to hear the speech of one other than You, after having heard You. Let it be known: no one is bound to a charge unless made able to bear it.

mots.racine: ر ب ب
ش ر ح / ص د ر

versets.ar: وَيَسِّرْ لِىٓ أَمْرِى
versets.tr: wa-yassir lī amrī
versets.trad: and make my task easy for me,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses, once he entered into colloquy with God, scarcely ceased asking, so pressed was he by the intimacy of the Word. “And ease for me my affair” — strengthen me to deliver what I must deliver by You, not by myself. The longing to ask is born of the sweetness of having been heard.

mots.racine: ي س ر / أ م ر

versets.ar: وَٱحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِى
versets.tr: wa-ḥlul ʿuqdatan min lisānī
versets.trad: and loosen the knot from my tongue
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “loosen the knot of my tongue” — that it may be freed to address one other than You. This tongue had tasted only the Word of the Real; it must be made supple to speak to creatures without spoiling what it received of the intimate discourse.

mots.racine: ح ل ل / ع ق د
ل س ن

versets.ar: يَفْقَهُوا۟ قَوْلِى
versets.tr: yafqahū qawlī
versets.trad: that they may understand my speech.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “that they may understand my words” — Moses asks for eloquence not for himself but for the entrusted cause. Just as the breast is opened and the tongue loosed, it is by God, not by oneself, that the servant transmits; he delivers what he delivers by Him. The word that illumines is a gift, not an acquisition.

mots.racine: ف ق ه / ق و ل

versets.ar: وَٱجْعَل لِّى وَزِيرًا مِّنْ أَهْلِى
versets.tr: wa-jʿal lī wazīran min ahlī
versets.trad: And appoint for me a helper from my family:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses asked to take his brother along, for going to creatures breeds desolation; he sought companionship to lighten the burden of the trial. But when he went to hear the Word of God, he went alone: love requires stripping bare and solitude, and no third has place beside the Beloved in the locus of intimacy.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / و ز ر
mots.def: wazīr: one who bears the load with another.

mots.racine: أ ه ل

versets.ar: هَٰرُونَ أَخِى
versets.tr: hārūna akhī
versets.trad: Aaron, my brother;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “Aaron, my brother” — Moses joins him to the mission toward men, where solitude weighs. Toward creatures he takes a companion; toward the tryst of the Word, no one has a path to his company, for the aim of that going was that he be singled out in his state.

mots.racine: أ خ و

versets.ar: ٱشْدُدْ بِهِۦٓ أَزْرِى
versets.tr: ushdud bihi azrī
versets.trad: by him strengthen me
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “strengthen through him my back” — brotherhood is a force for bearing the charge toward men. Yet Moses at once makes clear that it is not for his own portion that he asks this company: “that we may glorify You and remember You abundantly” — his request is by the right of his Lord, not by the share of his soul.

mots.racine: ش د د
أ ز ر

versets.ar: وَأَشْرِكْهُ فِىٓ أَمْرِى
versets.tr: wa-ashrik-hu fī amrī
versets.trad: and let him share my task
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: such is the mark of the faithful — their very companionship is offered to the Real. Moses showed that in seeking his brother's company he aimed only at God's right: “that we may glorify You and remember You abundantly.” What might seem a wish for human support is, in truth, an impulse of praise.

mots.racine: ش ر ك
أ م ر

versets.ar: كَىْ نُسَبِّحَكَ كَثِيرًا
versets.tr: kay nusabbiḥaka kathīran
versets.trad: that we may glorify You much
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “that we may glorify You abundantly” — the end of every request is glorification, not rest. Moses showed that his asking to share the charge with his brother was by his Lord's right and not by his soul's portion. The abundance of tasbīḥ is the true fruit one hopes for from companionship.

mots.racine: س ب ح / ك ث ر

versets.ar: وَنَذْكُرَكَ كَثِيرًا
versets.tr: wa-nadhkuraka kathīran
versets.trad: and remember You much.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “and that we may remember You abundantly” — abundant dhikr is the very reason help is asked. The servant seeks aid only that he may remember Him better, and constant remembrance is the end of every aid granted. Thus Moses's request closes upon praise, not upon gain.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ك ث ر

versets.ar: إِنَّكَ كُنتَ بِنَا بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: innaka kunta binā baṣīran
versets.trad: Truly, You are ever Seeing of us!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “your request is granted to you, O Moses” — and the Real at once recalls to him the forgotten origin of his state. “Have you forgotten your beginning, when We kept you in the river, saved your mother from anguish, and raised you in the lap of the enemy? Where then was your request, your choosing, your prayer?” He who lavished these graces upon you before any prayer is He who now grants your request: the favor precedes the merit.

mots.racine: ب ص ر

versets.ar: قَالَ قَدْ أُوتِيتَ سُؤْلَكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla qad ūtīta suʾlaka yā mūsā
versets.trad: He said: “O Moses, your request is granted!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the highest favor, for whoever knows his Lord, is that He forgive his faults. And all God granted Moses — the rescue in the river, the love cast upon him, the nurse restored — came before he asked for anything. The Real inscribed in the heart of Pharaoh's wife tenderness for him, and cast love upon him, until even his enemy loved him.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / س أ ل

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ مَنَنَّا عَلَيْكَ مَرَّةً أُخْرَىٰٓ
versets.tr: wa-laqad manannā ʿalayka marratan ukhrā
versets.trad: And indeed We bestowed a favor upon you once before,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “I cast upon you a love from Me” — when the Real loves a servant, every gaze that falls upon him loves him; He placed in the hearts of men the love of you. And more deeply: “I have fixed in your heart the love of Me,” for the servant's love for God is never anything but what the Real Himself deposits there. Love is a wonder cast upon you without cause.

mots.racine: م ن ن
م ر ر / أ خ ر

versets.ar: إِذْ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ مَا يُوحَىٰٓ
versets.tr: idh awḥaynā ilā ummika mā yūḥā
versets.trad: when We revealed to your mother what was revealed:
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it was a revelation of inspiration (ilhām), cast into the mother's heart. “That you be formed under My eye” — under My gaze; I allow no other to draw you far from Me. And: I guard you from every other, from every discourse but Ours; I entrusted your keeping to no one. Providence watches even before the child is aware.

mots.racine: و ح ي / أ م م

versets.ar: أَنِ ٱقْذِفِيهِ فِى ٱلتَّابُوتِ فَٱقْذِفِيهِ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ فَلْيُلْقِهِ ٱلْيَمُّ بِٱلسَّاحِلِ يَأْخُذْهُ عَدُوٌّ لِّى وَعَدُوٌّ لَّهُۥ ۚ وَأَلْقَيْتُ عَلَيْكَ مَحَبَّةً مِّنِّى وَلِتُصْنَعَ عَلَىٰ عَيْنِىٓ
versets.tr: ani qdhifīhi fī t-tābūti fa-qdhifīhi fī l-yammi fa-l-yulqihi l-yammu bi-s-sāḥili yaʾkhudhhu ʿaduwwun lī wa-ʿaduwwun lahu wa-alqaytu ʿalayka maḥabbatan minnī wa-li-tuṣnaʿa ʿalā ʿaynī
versets.trad: and I cast upon you a love from Me, that you might be fashioned under My eye.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses was raised in the lap of the enemy who, for his sake, had killed thousands of children — “but it is from his place of safety that what he dreads comes upon the cautious!” Pharaoh nursed him so that the ruin of his reign be accomplished by his hand: the secrets of the decrees, none knows but the All-Powerful. The love cast upon him was such that his very enemy became his protector.

mots.racine: ق ذ ف / ي م م
mots.def: qadhafa: to throw; yamm: sea, river.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ح ب ب
mots.sens: I cast upon you a love from Me

mots.racine: ص ن ع / ع ي ن
mots.sens: that you be fashioned under My eye

versets.ar: إِذْ تَمْشِىٓ أُخْتُكَ فَتَقُولُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ مَن يَكْفُلُهُۥ ۖ فَرَجَعْنَٰكَ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّكَ كَىْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُهَا وَلَا تَحْزَنَ ۚ وَقَتَلْتَ نَفْسًا فَنَجَّيْنَٰكَ مِنَ ٱلْغَمِّ وَفَتَنَّٰكَ فُتُونًا ۚ فَلَبِثْتَ سِنِينَ فِىٓ أَهْلِ مَدْيَنَ ثُمَّ جِئْتَ عَلَىٰ قَدَرٍ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: idh tamshī ukhtuka fa-taqūlu hal adullukum ʿalā man yakfuluhu fa-rajaʿnāka ilā ummika kay taqarra ʿaynuhā wa-lā taḥzana wa-qatalta nafsan fa-najjaynāka mina l-ghammi wa-fatannāka futūnan fa-labithta sinīna fī ahli madyana thumma jiʾta ʿalā qadarin yā mūsā
versets.trad: When your sister walked along and said: "Shall I show you one who will nurse him?" So We returned you to your mother, that her eye might be comforted and she not grieve. And you slew a man, yet We delivered you from distress and tried you with many trials. Then you stayed years among the people of Midian; then you came here at an appointed measure, O Moses!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the trial is according to the strength or weakness of the one who bears it: the stronger the man, the heavier his trial; the weaker he is, the lighter it is. Moses's mother was weak: her son was restored to her after a few days. Jacob was stronger: Joseph returned to him only after long years. And “We saved you from grief”: We made you see the eye of gathering (ʿayn al-jamʿ), so that the sorrow the dispersion inspired in you vanished.

mots.racine: ق ر ر / ع ي ن
mots.def: qarrat ʿaynuhu: to be consoled, gladdened.

mots.racine: ف ت ن
ج ي أ / ق د ر
mots.sens: you came according to a measure

versets.ar: وَٱصْطَنَعْتُكَ لِنَفْسِى
versets.tr: wa-ṣṭanaʿtuka li-nafsī
versets.trad: And I have fashioned you for Myself.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “I have fashioned you for Myself” — a word of the highest election. I have reserved you for Me alone, so that you befit no other; I have set apart your secret for Me, turned your impulse toward Me alone, and raised a barrier between you and all that is below Me. Thus cut off from everything, he was then told: “go to Pharaoh.”

mots.racine: ص ن ع / ن ف س
mots.def: iṣṭanaʿa: to choose, to fashion for oneself.

versets.ar: ٱذْهَبْ أَنتَ وَأَخُوكَ بِـَٔايَٰتِى وَلَا تَنِيَا فِى ذِكْرِى
versets.tr: idhhab anta wa-akhūka bi-āyātī wa-lā taniyā fī dhikrī
versets.trad: Go, you and your brother, with My signs, and do not slacken in My remembrance.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “and do not slacken in My remembrance” — constant recollection is the weapon of the envoy sent among creatures. Moses heaped up excuses before the sending to Pharaoh, but to no avail: the Word “I am with you both, I hear and I see” sufficed him, and he said: now, since You are with me, I care for nothing.

mots.racine: أ ي ي
و ن ي / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: do not slacken in My remembrance

versets.ar: ٱذْهَبَآ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُۥ طَغَىٰ
versets.tr: idhhabā ilā firʿawna innahu ṭaghā
versets.trad: Go, both of you, to Pharaoh, for he has transgressed;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses pleaded many excuses — “my breast is straitened, my tongue does not loosen,” “I killed one of them and I fear they will kill me.” None of these pretexts was granted him; but when God said: “I am with you both, I hear and I see,” Moses found himself wholly sufficed, and every fear fell away. The presence of the Real dispenses with every other guarantee.

mots.racine: ذ ه ب
ط غ ي

versets.ar: فَقُولَا لَهُۥ قَوْلًا لَّيِّنًا لَّعَلَّهُۥ يَتَذَكَّرُ أَوْ يَخْشَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-qūlā lahu qawlan layyinan laʿallahu yatadhakkaru aw yakhshā
versets.trad: and speak to him a gentle word: perhaps he will be mindful, or will fear.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from the command of gentleness pearls in cascade: if such is gentleness toward one who denies Him, what is it toward one who proclaims Him One? If such is His regard for the unbelievers, what is it for the people of good? If such is His gentleness toward one who says “I,” what is it toward one who says “You”? Gentleness was prescribed because it is the time of respite, when one waits for the other to reflect.

mots.racine: ق و ل / ل ي ن
ذ ك ر / خ ش ي
mots.def: tadhakkara: to remember; khashiya: to fear.

versets.ar: قَالَا رَبَّنَآ إِنَّنَا نَخَافُ أَن يَفْرُطَ عَلَيْنَآ أَوْ أَن يَطْغَىٰ
versets.tr: qālā rabbanā innanā nakhāfu an yafruṭa ʿalaynā aw an yaṭghā
versets.trad: They said: “Our Lord! We fear that he may rush upon us or transgress.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: there is here proof that the fear human nature requires does not expose its subject to blame, since men such as Moses and Aaron said: “we fear.” Yet it is also said: they did not fear for themselves; they feared lest a stratagem of Pharaoh hinder the carrying-out of Your command — their fear was for God's right, not for the portion of their souls.

mots.racine: خ و ف
ف ر ط / ط غ ي
mots.sens: that he rush upon us or transgress
mots.def: farraṭa: to be hasty; ṭaghā: to exceed.

versets.ar: قَالَ لَا تَخَافَآ ۖ إِنَّنِى مَعَكُمَآ أَسْمَعُ وَأَرَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla lā takhāfā innanī maʿakumā asmaʿu wa-arā
versets.trad: He said: “Fear not! I am with you both; I hear and I see.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: by their “we fear,” they knew how to draw from the Real the “I am with you both.” And that was indeed their aim: that the Real say to them this word — else, whence would fear come to one distinguished by prophethood? He stilled the fear in them by “I am with you both,” and they were strengthened, for the condition of every charge is the enabling for it.

mots.racine: خ و ف
س م ع / ر أ ي
mots.sens: with you, I hear and I see

versets.ar: فَأْتِيَاهُ فَقُولَآ إِنَّا رَسُولَا رَبِّكَ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعَنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَلَا تُعَذِّبْهُمْ ۖ قَدْ جِئْنَٰكَ بِـَٔايَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكَ ۖ وَٱلسَّلَٰمُ عَلَىٰ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ
versets.tr: fa-ʾtiyāhu fa-qūlā innā rasūlā rabbika fa-arsil maʿanā banī isrāʾīla wa-lā tuʿadhdhibhum qad jiʾnāka bi-āyatin min rabbika wa-s-salāmu ʿalā mani ttabaʿa l-hudā
versets.trad: So go to him and say: “We are two messengers of your Lord. Send forth with us the Children of Israel and do not torment them. We have come to you with a sign from your Lord. And peace be upon whoever follows the guidance!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the trial had grown long for the Children of Israel through Pharaoh, and the Real succored them, even after a time: such is His custom, that He gives rein to the wrongdoer, but when He seizes him, His grip is painful. “Peace be upon whoever follows guidance”: only he follows guidance whose heart has been anointed with the collyrium of the light of gnosis; but upon whom lies the veil of ignorance, when would he hearken to guidance?

mots.racine: ر س ل / ر ب ب
س ل م / ه د ي

versets.ar: إِنَّا قَدْ أُوحِىَ إِلَيْنَآ أَنَّ ٱلْعَذَابَ عَلَىٰ مَن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ
versets.tr: innā qad ūḥiya ilaynā anna l-ʿadhāba ʿalā man kadhdhaba wa-tawallā
versets.trad: It has been revealed to us that the punishment will fall upon whoever denies and turns away."”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God sent no prophet without his warning his people of the punishment for forsaking the command and announcing the reward for keeping it. Punishment is of two kinds, hastened and deferred; the deferred, no enemy knows its detail, as none knows the detail of the reserved reward: “no soul knows what is hidden for it of coolness of the eyes.”

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ك ذ ب
و ل ي

versets.ar: قَالَ فَمَن رَّبُّكُمَا يَٰمُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla fa-man rabbukumā yā mūsā
versets.trad: Pharaoh said: “And who is your Lord, O Moses?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to the question “who then is your Lord?” Pharaoh disputed about the name; but the deeper allusion concerns the hastened punishment, which takes many forms. Hardness of heart is a kind of punishment; the heedlessness that creeps into obedience is another; the loss of a portion in wealth and souls is yet another — each according to his station, for the worsening of the penalty signals the elevation of the rank.

mots.racine: ر ب ب

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبُّنَا ٱلَّذِىٓ أَعْطَىٰ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ خَلْقَهُۥ ثُمَّ هَدَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla rabbunā lladhī aʿṭā kulla shayʾin khalqahu thumma hadā
versets.trad: He replied: “Our Lord is He who gave to each thing its created nature, then guided it.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses answered not by the name but by inference from the acts: “our Lord is He who gave each thing its creation, then guided it” — that one may know the proof of His being is what His acts indicate. One points to the Giver only by showing the gift, and each creature endowed with its form attests the One who fashioned and directed it.

mots.racine: ع ط و / خ ل ق
mots.sens: He gave each thing its creation
mots.def: aʿṭā: to give; khalq: nature, form.

mots.racine: ه د ي

versets.ar: قَالَ فَمَا بَالُ ٱلْقُرُونِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla fa-mā bālu l-qurūni l-ūlā
versets.trad: He asked: “And what of the former generations?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to “what of the former generations?” Moses answers: “their knowledge is with my Lord, in a Book.” And the sense of the ishāra: I can inform you only of what my Lord has informed me; what He has made me know, I make known; what He has veiled from me, I stop short before. The knower never outruns his Lord's gift.

mots.racine: ق ر ن / أ و ل

versets.ar: قَالَ عِلْمُهَا عِندَ رَبِّى فِى كِتَٰبٍ ۖ لَّا يَضِلُّ رَبِّى وَلَا يَنسَى
versets.tr: qāla ʿilmuhā ʿinda rabbī fī kitābin lā yaḍillu rabbī wa-lā yansā
versets.trad: He said: “Their knowledge is with my Lord, in a Book. My Lord neither errs nor forgets.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “He neither strays nor forgets” — and the servant, in His image, lays claim to knowledge only to the measure of unveiling. What the Real has made me know, I say; what He has kept secret, I stop short before. Silence about the unknown is itself a knowledge, and the avowal of ignorance a station of courtesy toward God.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ك ت ب
mots.sens: their knowledge is with my Lord, in a Book

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ن س ي
mots.sens: my Lord neither errs nor forgets
mots.def: ḍalla: to stray; nasiya: to forget.

versets.ar: ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ مَهْدًا وَسَلَكَ لَكُمْ فِيهَا سُبُلًا وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزْوَٰجًا مِّن نَّبَاتٍ شَتَّىٰ
versets.tr: alladhī jaʿala lakumu l-arḍa mahdan wa-salaka lakum fīhā subulan wa-anzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-akhrajnā bihi azwājan min nabātin shattā
versets.trad: It is He who made the earth a cradle for you, traced paths in it for you, and sent down water from the sky, with which We bring forth pairs of diverse plants.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the ascent of dwellings: He made the earth a rest for their bodies, and their bodies a dwelling for His worship; their hearts a dwelling for His gnosis, their spirits a dwelling for His love, and their secrets a dwelling for His contemplation. The rain that brings forth pairs of plants has its inner counterpart: each abode of man receives its water and bears its fruit.

mots.racine: أ ر ض / م ه د
ز و ج / ن ب ت

versets.ar: كُلُوا۟ وَٱرْعَوْا۟ أَنْعَٰمَكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَٰتٍ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلنُّهَىٰ
versets.tr: kulū wa-rʿaw anʿāmakum inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-ulī n-nuhā
versets.trad: Eat and pasture your cattle! In that are signs for those endowed with understanding.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He has prepared for them the means of livelihood, and as He provided for them, He provided for the beasts from which they draw benefit. He commands them to strengthen themselves with what their hands reach and to profit from their flocks, as much as they can, that His grace upon them be perfected. Therein are signs for those endowed with reason.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ر ع ي
ن ه ي

versets.ar: مِنْهَا خَلَقْنَٰكُمْ وَفِيهَا نُعِيدُكُمْ وَمِنْهَا نُخْرِجُكُمْ تَارَةً أُخْرَىٰ
versets.tr: minhā khalaqnākum wa-fīhā nuʿīdukum wa-minhā nukhrijukum tāratan ukhrā
versets.trad: From it We created you, into it We return you, and from it We will bring you forth once again.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “from it We created you, into it We return you” — and he draws from it a secret of bodies and spirits: the bodies are molds and the spirits deposits; the lot of the molds is the earth, the attribute of the deposits is nearness. The molds He adorns with His favors; the deposits He revives by the unveiling of His majesty and the gentleness of His beauty.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
خ ر ج
mots.sens: We bring you forth another time
mots.def: akhraja: to bring forth; tāra: time.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرَيْنَٰهُ ءَايَٰتِنَا كُلَّهَا فَكَذَّبَ وَأَبَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-laqad araynāhu āyātinā kullahā fa-kadhdhaba wa-abā
versets.trad: And We showed Pharaoh all Our signs, but he denied and refused.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He commanded him to proclaim openly, and blinded him in his secret to the vision of what he proclaimed — that is, Pharaoh, to whom the signs were all shown, who denied and refused. Neither the Word penetrated him, nor the warning of punishment, nor the eased grace profited him: the veil upon the heart makes every sign of no avail.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / أ ي ي
mots.sens: We showed him all Our signs

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / أ ب ي

versets.ar: قَالَ أَجِئْتَنَا لِتُخْرِجَنَا مِنْ أَرْضِنَا بِسِحْرِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla a-jiʾtanā li-tukhrijanā min arḍinā bi-siḥrika yā mūsā
versets.trad: He said: “Have you come to us, O Moses, to drive us from our land by your magic?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses called them to God and spoke to them of the hereafter, by the announcement of reward and the threat of punishment; but they responded only from the side of this world, and the more they were exhorted, the more they grew in heedlessness and ignorance. They saw in the wonder only sorcery, for the heart turned toward the earth perceives only the earth.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / خ ر ج
mots.sens: have you come to drive us out

mots.racine: س ح ر

versets.ar: فَلَنَأْتِيَنَّكَ بِسِحْرٍ مِّثْلِهِۦ فَٱجْعَلْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكَ مَوْعِدًا لَّا نُخْلِفُهُۥ نَحْنُ وَلَآ أَنتَ مَكَانًا سُوًى
versets.tr: fa-la-naʾtiyannaka bi-siḥrin mithlihi fa-jʿal baynanā wa-baynaka mawʿidan lā nukhlifuhu naḥnu wa-lā anta makānan suwan
versets.trad: We shall bring you magic like it. So set between us and you an appointment that neither we nor you will fail, in a fair place.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “fix between us a meeting” — they made ready to rise against the Reality and rolled up their sleeves for opposition; but the Will broke them and the Power crushed them. Such is the one the Real has marked with distance: no gnosis, no true faith, no regret for what escapes him, no assent to the reality with which he busies himself.

mots.racine: س ح ر / م ث ل
و ع د / س و ي

versets.ar: قَالَ مَوْعِدُكُمْ يَوْمُ ٱلزِّينَةِ وَأَن يُحْشَرَ ٱلنَّاسُ ضُحًى
versets.tr: qāla mawʿidukum yawmu z-zīnati wa-an yuḥshara n-nāsu ḍuḥan
versets.trad: Moses said: “Let your appointment be the day of the festival, and let the people be gathered in the forenoon!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “your meeting is the day of adornment (yawm al-zīna)” — and that day was the day of their disgrace. Whoever adorns himself with what is not in him, the testimonies of trial unmask him. The day of outward ornament became the day on which empty appearance was confounded by the Reality.

mots.racine: ز ي ن
mots.sens: the day of adornment, the festival

mots.racine: ح ش ر / ض ح و
mots.sens: that the people be gathered in forenoon

versets.ar: فَتَوَلَّىٰ فِرْعَوْنُ فَجَمَعَ كَيْدَهُۥ ثُمَّ أَتَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-tawallā firʿawnu fa-jamaʿa kaydahu thumma atā
versets.trad: So Pharaoh withdrew; he gathered his cunning, then he came.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “Pharaoh turned away, gathered his stratagem, then came” — he schemed and was schemed against, he willed and it turned back upon him, he called for assembly and was humiliated. Moses left nothing of exhortation and gentleness; Pharaoh left nothing of foolishness and madness. The creature's cunning shatters upon the Real's mastery.

mots.racine: ج م ع / ك ي د
أ ت ي

versets.ar: قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰ وَيْلَكُمْ لَا تَفْتَرُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا فَيُسْحِتَكُم بِعَذَابٍ ۖ وَقَدْ خَابَ مَنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla lahum mūsā waylakum lā taftarū ʿalā llāhi kadhiban fa-yusḥitakum bi-ʿadhābin wa-qad khāba mani ftarā
versets.trad: Moses said to them: “Woe to you! Do not forge a lie against God, lest He destroy you with a punishment. He who forges a lie has failed!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses warned them — “do not forge a lie against God, lest He exterminate you with a punishment.” Know that no one has any strength against God when He chastises. But they held his word for imposture and struck his wonder with the name of sorcery: frustrated is whoever forges the lie, for slander against the Truth turns first against the one who utters it.

mots.racine: ف ر ي / ك ذ ب
mots.sens: do not forge a lie against God

mots.racine: خ ي ب / ف ر ي
mots.def: khāba: to be disappointed, to lose.

versets.ar: فَتَنَٰزَعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَهُم بَيْنَهُمْ وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّجْوَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-tanāzaʿū amrahum baynahum wa-asarrū n-najwā
versets.trad: So they disputed their affair among themselves and kept their counsel secret.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “they disputed their affair and kept their counsel secret” — already doubt worked at the magicians. They held Moses's word for imposture and named his wonder sorcery; but the secret of their inner council foretold the reversal in which, defeated, they would prostrate themselves. The outward dispute covered the crack through which the light was about to enter.

mots.racine: ن ز ع
س ر ر / ن ج و

versets.ar: قَالُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَٰذَٰنِ لَسَٰحِرَٰنِ يُرِيدَانِ أَن يُخْرِجَاكُم مِّنْ أَرْضِكُم بِسِحْرِهِمَا وَيَذْهَبَا بِطَرِيقَتِكُمُ ٱلْمُثْلَىٰ
versets.tr: qālū in hādhāni la-sāḥirāni yurīdāni an yukhrijākum min arḍikum bi-siḥrihimā wa-yadhhabā bi-ṭarīqatikumu l-muthlā
versets.trad: They said: “These two are surely magicians who wish to drive you from your land by their sorcery and to do away with your exemplary way of life.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “these two are sorcerers” — in their claim they are liars, aiming to drive you from your land and to cast confusion into your belief. So speaks one who takes the Truth for sorcery: he accuses the other of what his own heart is full of, and calls a threat what is in reality his salvation offered.

mots.racine: س ح ر
ط ر ق / م ث ل

versets.ar: فَأَجْمِعُوا۟ كَيْدَكُمْ ثُمَّ ٱئْتُوا۟ صَفًّا ۚ وَقَدْ أَفْلَحَ ٱلْيَوْمَ مَنِ ٱسْتَعْلَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-ajmiʿū kaydakum thumma ʾtū ṣaffan wa-qad aflaḥa l-yawma mani staʿlā
versets.trad: So gather your cunning, then come in ranks. And he who prevails today is the one who will be happy!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “gather your stratagem, then come in rank” — they paraded their bravado, believing victory theirs, abandoning themselves to what the sorcerers held out to them. “He has succeeded today who prevails”: but the success they reckoned on through cunning was only the threshold of their defeat, for no one prevails who rises against the Truth.

mots.racine: ج م ع / ك ي د
ف ل ح / ع ل و
mots.def: aflaḥa: to succeed; istaʿlā: to prevail.

versets.ar: قَالُوا۟ يَٰمُوسَىٰٓ إِمَّآ أَن تُلْقِىَ وَإِمَّآ أَن نَّكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَلْقَىٰ
versets.tr: qālū yā mūsā immā an tulqiya wa-immā an nakūna awwala man alqā
versets.trad: They said: “O Moses! Either you cast, or shall we be the first to cast?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “O Moses, will you cast, or shall we be the first to cast?” — they displayed their assurance, believing victory secured, and offered Moses to begin, trusting in their illusion. But this boldness was the extreme of blindness: he who challenges the Truth in the full confidence of his cunning is the nearest to his fall.

mots.racine: ل ق ي
أ و ل / ل ق ي

versets.ar: قَالَ بَلْ أَلْقُوا۟ ۖ فَإِذَا حِبَالُهُمْ وَعِصِيُّهُمْ يُخَيَّلُ إِلَيْهِ مِن سِحْرِهِمْ أَنَّهَا تَسْعَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla bal alqū fa-idhā ḥibāluhum wa-ʿiṣiyyuhum yukhayyalu ilayhi min siḥrihim annahā tasʿā
versets.trad: He said: “No, you cast!” And behold, by their magic their ropes and staffs seemed to him to be gliding.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Moses said “cast, you” — not to permit sorcery, but because the Real willed to unveil their trickery. When they made it appear to the people that the ropes were serpents, and Moses's staff swallowed all their work, the magicians recognized it was a heavenly matter, the moment they saw the heap of their ropes vanish and the serpent become a staff again.

mots.racine: ح ب ل / ع ص و
خ ي ل / س ع ي
mots.sens: it seemed to him they were gliding

versets.ar: فَأَوْجَسَ فِى نَفْسِهِۦ خِيفَةً مُّوسَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-awjasa fī nafsihi khīfatan mūsā
versets.trad: And Moses conceived a secret fear within himself.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “Moses conceived within himself a fear” — the fear that pertains to human nature exposes no one to blame. Even the prophet feels the instinctive apprehension; but the Real at once stills it. This passing tremor does not impair the station: it is the reminder that the servant, even a prophet, remains a servant, and that steadfastness comes from God alone.

mots.racine: و ج س / ن ف س
خ و ف

versets.ar: قُلْنَا لَا تَخَفْ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ
versets.tr: qulnā lā takhaf innaka anta l-aʿlā
versets.trad: We said: “Fear not! It is you who will have the upper hand!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “do not fear, you are the highest” — the Word came to still the tremor of nature. Just as the Real steadies His servants through the variation of states, taking and giving back, He comes here to re-strengthen the heart seized with fear. Security is born not of one's own strength but of the assurance received: “you are the highest.”

mots.racine: خ و ف
ع ل و
mots.sens: it is you who are uppermost

versets.ar: وَأَلْقِ مَا فِى يَمِينِكَ تَلْقَفْ مَا صَنَعُوٓا۟ ۖ إِنَّمَا صَنَعُوا۟ كَيْدُ سَٰحِرٍ ۖ وَلَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلسَّاحِرُ حَيْثُ أَتَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-alqi mā fī yamīnika talqaf mā ṣanaʿū innamā ṣanaʿū kaydu sāḥirin wa-lā yufliḥu s-sāḥiru ḥaythu atā
versets.trad: Cast what is in your right hand: it will swallow what they have made, for what they made is only a magician's trick. And the magician does not prosper wherever he comes.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “cast what is in your right hand, it will swallow what they have made” — what they have made is but a sorcerer's stratagem, and the sorcerer does not succeed, wherever he goes. The staff cast by command is no longer Moses's prop but the Real's instrument: what was abandoned for God returns to annihilate the imposture. Cunning collapses the moment the Truth extends its hand.

mots.racine: ل ق ف / ص ن ع
ف ل ح / س ح ر

versets.ar: فَأُلْقِىَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ سُجَّدًا قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّ هَٰرُونَ وَمُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-ulqiya s-saḥaratu sujjadan qālū āmannā bi-rabbi hārūna wa-mūsā
versets.trad: So the magicians were cast down in prostration, saying: “We believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the suns of gnosis rose in their secrets and the lights of solicitude spread over them, the magicians saw the Real by their secrets. Then they fell prostrate, openly declaring their faith: “we believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses.” What had begun in dispute ended in the light entered all at once into the heart.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / س ج د
mots.sens: the magicians were cast down prostrate

mots.racine: أ م ن / ر ب ب
mots.sens: we believe in the Lord of Aaron and Moses

versets.ar: قَالَ ءَامَنتُمْ لَهُۥ قَبْلَ أَنْ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ٱلَّذِى عَلَّمَكُمُ ٱلسِّحْرَ ۖ فَلَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُم مِّنْ خِلَٰفٍ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ فِى جُذُوعِ ٱلنَّخْلِ وَلَتَعْلَمُنَّ أَيُّنَآ أَشَدُّ عَذَابًا وَأَبْقَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla āmantum lahu qabla an ādhana lakum innahu la-kabīrukumu lladhī ʿallamakumu s-siḥra fa-la-uqaṭṭiʿanna aydiyakum wa-arjulakum min khilāfin wa-la-uṣallibannakum fī judhūʿi n-nakhli wa-la-taʿlamunna ayyunā ashaddu ʿadhāban wa-abqā
versets.trad: Pharaoh said: “You have believed in him before I gave you leave! He must be your master, who taught you magic. I will surely cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, then crucify you on the trunks of palm trees. And you will surely know which of us is harsher in punishment and more lasting!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Pharaoh threatened them with death and the cross, with all sorts of terrible torments; but whoever had lately sworn by Pharaoh's might began to swear by God. The axis of hearts had tipped: the threat of punishment weighed nothing against the witnessing received. In the morning they were unbelieving sorcerers, in the evening they were chosen and pious — such is the short distance that separates unbelief from faith, like morning from evening.

mots.ar: ءَامَنتُمْ لَهُۥ قَبْلَ أَنْ ءَاذَنَ لَكُمْ
mots.tr: āmantum lahu qabla an ādhana lakum
mots.racine: أ م ن / أ ذ ن
ق ط ع / خ ل ف
mots.sens: I will cut off hands and feet on opposite sides

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ذ ب / ب ق ي
mots.sens: harsher in punishment and more lasting

versets.ar: قَالُوا۟ لَن نُّؤْثِرَكَ عَلَىٰ مَا جَآءَنَا مِنَ ٱلْبَيِّنَٰتِ وَٱلَّذِى فَطَرَنَا ۖ فَٱقْضِ مَآ أَنتَ قَاضٍ ۖ إِنَّمَا تَقْضِى هَٰذِهِ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَآ
versets.tr: qālū lan nuʾthiraka ʿalā mā jāʾanā mina l-bayyināti wa-lladhī faṭaranā fa-qḍi mā anta qāḍin innamā taqḍī hādhihi l-ḥayāta d-dunyā
versets.trad: They said: “We will never prefer you over the clear proofs that have come to us, nor over Him who created us. So decree what you will decree: you decree only for this worldly life!
versets.commentaire: The converted magicians do not retreat. Qushayrī: when the suns of gnosis rose in their secrets and the lights of solicitude spread over them, they beheld the Real with their hearts and proclaimed their faith; they prostrated inwardly before the One they contemplated, no longer frightened by the threatened tortures. They found the trial sweet and bore its weight — in the morning unbelieving sorcerers, in the evening chosen and righteous. And they knew that the trial of this world comes to an end, however prolonged, and ceases, however extreme.

mots.racine: أ ث ر
ج ي أ / ب ي ن
mots.sens: the proofs that have come to us

mots.racine: ق ض ي

versets.ar: إِنَّآ ءَامَنَّا بِرَبِّنَا لِيَغْفِرَ لَنَا خَطَٰيَٰنَا وَمَآ أَكْرَهْتَنَا عَلَيْهِ مِنَ ٱلسِّحْرِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰ
versets.tr: innā āmannā bi-rabbinā li-yaghfira lanā khaṭāyānā wa-mā akrahtanā ʿalayhi mina s-siḥri wa-llāhu khayrun wa-abqā
versets.trad: We have believed in our Lord, that He may forgive us our sins and the sorcery to which you compelled us. God is better and more lasting!”
versets.commentaire: Their first desire, scarcely converted, is forgiveness. Qushayrī: for whoever knows God, the most important thing is that He forgive his sins. Thus Adam, when his state was unveiled and what befell befell, said: “My Lord, I have wronged myself”; and the Prophet himself implored forgiveness, saying that a veil would pass over his heart and he would turn to God many times a day. Forgiveness is the first thirst of the heart awakening to its Lord. And their word “God is better and more lasting” opposes the permanence of the Real to the fragility of every threat.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / خ ط أ
mots.sens: that He forgive us our sins

mots.racine: خ ي ر / ب ق ي
mots.sens: God is better and more lasting

versets.ar: إِنَّهُۥ مَن يَأْتِ رَبَّهُۥ مُجْرِمًا فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ جَهَنَّمَ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحْيَىٰ
versets.tr: innahu man yaʾti rabbahu mujriman fa-inna lahu jahannama lā yamūtu fīhā wa-lā yaḥyā
versets.trad: Indeed, whoever comes to his Lord as a criminal, for him is Hell, wherein he neither dies nor lives.
versets.commentaire: The criminal knows there neither a death that delivers nor a life that consoles. Qushayrī, in the register of the sūra, sees in this in-between the worst of states: suspended between a refused death and a denied life, the criminal remains in deprivation. The true torment is not only the fire, but the absence of all rest — neither term nor relief.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ج ر م
mots.sens: comes to his Lord as a criminal

mots.racine: م و ت / ح ي ي
mots.sens: he neither dies nor lives therein
mots.def: māta: to die; ḥayya: to live.

versets.ar: وَمَن يَأْتِهِۦ مُؤْمِنًا قَدْ عَمِلَ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ فَأُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ لَهُمُ ٱلدَّرَجَٰتُ ٱلْعُلَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-man yaʾtihi muʾminan qad ʿamila ṣ-ṣāliḥāti fa-ulāʾika lahumu d-darajātu l-ʿulā
versets.trad: But whoever comes to Him a believer, having done righteous deeds — for them are the highest degrees:
versets.commentaire: To the criminal answers the believer who works, promised the highest degrees. Qushayrī, in the spirit of the sūra: the “highest degrees” are not measured by the quantity of deeds but by the purity of the faith that animates them; the supreme ranks are for those whose heart, coming to God, bears nothing but Him.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / أ م ن
د ر ج / ع ل و

versets.ar: جَنَّٰتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ جَزَآءُ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ
versets.tr: jannātu ʿadnin tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā wa-dhālika jazāʾu man tazakkā
versets.trad: the Gardens of Eden, beneath which rivers flow. They abide therein forever. Such is the reward of whoever purifies himself.
versets.commentaire: The supreme reward goes to him who has purified himself. Qushayrī, in the register of the sūra: “the reward of whoever purifies himself” (man tazakkā) — the purification of the heart from all that is not God is the condition of the eternal abode; the Gardens of Eden are for one who has washed himself not only of fault but of attachment to self.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع د ن
ج ز ي / ز ك و
mots.sens: the reward of whoever purifies himself

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبَادِى فَٱضْرِبْ لَهُمْ طَرِيقًا فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ يَبَسًا لَّا تَخَٰفُ دَرَكًا وَلَا تَخْشَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-laqad awḥaynā ilā mūsā an asri bi-ʿibādī fa-ḍrib lahum ṭarīqan fī l-baḥri yabasan lā takhāfu darakan wa-lā takhshā
versets.trad: We revealed to Moses: “Travel by night with My servants. Strike for them a dry path through the sea. Fear neither being overtaken nor anything else!”
versets.commentaire: A dry path opens in the sea for God's servants. Qushayrī: when Moses led the Children of Israel across the sea and Pharaoh drew near, seeing the sea split and the path within it dry, he deceived his people, claiming it had opened by his own majesty, even saying “I am your Lord most high.” The sea obeys the divine order, and the proud man reads in it the opposite of the truth.

mots.racine: س ر ي / ع ب د
mots.sens: travel by night with My servants

mots.racine: ط ر ق / ب ح ر / ي ب س
mots.sens: a dry path through the sea

mots.racine: خ و ف / د ر ك

versets.ar: فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ بِجُنُودِهِۦ فَغَشِيَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْيَمِّ مَا غَشِيَهُمْ
versets.tr: fa-atbaʿahum firʿawnu bi-junūdihi fa-ghashiyahum mina l-yammi mā ghashiyahum
versets.trad: Then Pharaoh pursued them with his armies, but there overwhelmed them of the sea what overwhelmed them.
versets.commentaire: The waters cover Pharaoh and his troops. Qushayrī: Pharaoh entered the sea with his army down to the last man, and when the first sought to come out, God commanded the waves to close; they were all engulfed. He believed at the moment despair appeared, but his acknowledgment availed him nothing — it would have saved him without his obstinacy. The wretchedness decreed for him by destiny had overtaken him.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ج ن د
mots.sens: Pharaoh pursued them with his armies

mots.racine: غ ش ي / ي م م
mots.sens: the flood covered them what covered them

versets.ar: وَأَضَلَّ فِرْعَوْنُ قَوْمَهُۥ وَمَا هَدَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-aḍalla firʿawnu qawmahu wa-mā hadā
versets.trad: And Pharaoh led his people astray and did not guide them.
versets.commentaire: The false guide does not stray alone: he drags his people with him. Qushayrī, in the spirit of the sūra: Pharaoh claimed to lead and only lost; he who sets himself up as lord without the truth leads his own to the abyss. Guidance belongs to God alone — he who falsely claims it offers only straying.

mots.racine: ض ل ل
ه د ي

versets.ar: يَٰبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ قَدْ أَنجَيْنَٰكُم مِّنْ عَدُوِّكُمْ وَوَٰعَدْنَٰكُمْ جَانِبَ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلْأَيْمَنَ وَنَزَّلْنَا عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْمَنَّ وَٱلسَّلْوَىٰ
versets.tr: yā banī isrāʾīla qad anjaynākum min ʿaduwwikum wa-wāʿadnākum jāniba ṭ-ṭūri l-aymana wa-nazzalnā ʿalaykumu l-manna wa-s-salwā
versets.trad: O Children of Israel! We delivered you from your enemy, We appointed for you the right side of the Mount [Sinai], and We sent down upon you the manna and the quails,
versets.commentaire: God enumerates His graces to the Children of Israel. Qushayrī: He reminds them of His blessings and counts His favors, commanding them to cling to obedience and to fulfill gratitude for the abundance of gifts poured upon them; then He recalls what He bestowed upon their forefathers — the descent of manna and quails, but also the trials and diverse afflictions. The reminder of the blessing is inseparable from the call to gratitude.

mots.racine: ن ج و / ع د و
mots.sens: We saved you from your enemy

mots.racine: م ن ن / س ل و

versets.ar: كُلُوا۟ مِن طَيِّبَٰتِ مَا رَزَقْنَٰكُمْ وَلَا تَطْغَوْا۟ فِيهِ فَيَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ غَضَبِى ۖ وَمَن يَحْلِلْ عَلَيْهِ غَضَبِى فَقَدْ هَوَىٰ
versets.tr: kulū min ṭayyibāti mā razaqnākum wa-lā taṭghaw fīhi fa-yaḥilla ʿalaykum ghaḍabī wa-man yaḥlil ʿalayhi ghaḍabī fa-qad hawā
versets.trad: [saying:] “Eat of the good things We have provided for you, but do not transgress therein, lest My wrath descend upon you. And he upon whom My wrath descends has fallen!
versets.commentaire: The lawful (ṭayyib) is defined in several ways. Qushayrī: the good in provision is what is lawful; it is also said: that whose acquiring has not disobeyed God; or what is received in the contemplation of the Provider; or that from which gratitude is born. And “do not transgress” means: pass not from the lawful to the unlawful, exceed not the necessary, eat not in heedlessness and forgetfulness. Provisions differ: to some the shares of the soul, to others the rights of the heart, to others still the contemplation of secrets.

mots.racine: ط ي ب / ر ز ق
mots.sens: the good things We provide you

mots.racine: ط غ ي
ح ل ل / غ ض ب / ه و ي
mots.sens: upon whom My wrath falls, he has fallen

versets.ar: وَإِنِّى لَغَفَّارٌ لِّمَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَٰلِحًا ثُمَّ ٱهْتَدَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-innī la-ghaffārun li-man tāba wa-āmana wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan thumma htadā
versets.trad: And I am surely Most Forgiving toward whoever repents, believes, does righteous deeds, and then is rightly guided.”
versets.commentaire: Ghaffār: He whose forgiveness is immense. Qushayrī: from you a repentance of a single fault, and from Him the forgiveness of countless sins — including those that none but He knows, whose secret even the angels ignore. It is also said: “then was rightly guided” means he no longer saw his deeds as coming from himself, believing every event comes from the Real. True tawḥīd is the abandonment of the “I”s and the negation of every claim of the soul. And “he was guided,” that is: he guided himself toward Us by Us.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ت و ب
mots.def: ghaffār: who forgives endlessly; tāba: to repent.

mots.racine: ه د ي

versets.ar: وَمَآ أَعْجَلَكَ عَن قَوْمِكَ يَٰمُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-mā aʿjalaka ʿan qawmika yā mūsā
versets.trad: “And what made you hasten ahead of your people, O Moses?”
versets.commentaire: God questions Moses about his haste. Qushayrī: he had brought his people with him, then outstripped them by a few steps, so they fell behind; he was then addressed with this reproach out of regard for the right of their companionship. Some are blamed for their lateness, others for their forwardness — how different they are! Even haste toward God may fail the right of the companion.

mots.racine: ع ج ل
ق و م

versets.ar: قَالَ هُمْ أُو۟لَآءِ عَلَىٰٓ أَثَرِى وَعَجِلْتُ إِلَيْكَ رَبِّ لِتَرْضَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla hum ulāʾi ʿalā atharī wa-ʿajiltu ilayka rabbi li-tarḍā
versets.trad: He replied: “They are close upon my tracks, and I hastened to You, my Lord, that You may be pleased!”
versets.commentaire: Moses confesses: he hastened out of desire for the divine pleasure. Qushayrī: “I hastened to You,” that is, out of longing for You — and it was God who drew this confession from him, for had He not made him speak, Moses would not have said it. Then the Real teaches him: “My pleasure is that you be with them and not outstrip them; your presence with the weak whom you accompany pleases Me more than your going ahead of them.”

mots.racine: أ ث ر
mots.sens: they are here upon my tracks

mots.racine: ع ج ل / ر ض و
mots.sens: I hastened to You for Your pleasure

versets.ar: قَالَ فَإِنَّا قَدْ فَتَنَّا قَوْمَكَ مِنۢ بَعْدِكَ وَأَضَلَّهُمُ ٱلسَّامِرِىُّ
versets.tr: qāla fa-innā qad fatannā qawmaka min baʿdika wa-aḍallahumu s-sāmiriyyu
versets.trad: He said: “Indeed, We have tried your people after you, and the Sāmirī has led them astray.”
versets.commentaire: God announces that the people have been tried and led astray. Qushayrī: “We have tried your people” — He teaches that this came from Him, by decree, which refutes whoever denies destiny. Moses sought the Real's pleasure, and the Real decreed his people's trial; the judgment belongs to God, and Moses had no recourse but to be content with the divine decree — no objection against God — knowing He does what He wills.

mots.racine: ف ت ن
mots.sens: We tried your people after you

mots.racine: ض ل ل

versets.ar: فَرَجَعَ مُوسَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِۦ غَضْبَٰنَ أَسِفًا ۚ قَالَ يَٰقَوْمِ أَلَمْ يَعِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ وَعْدًا حَسَنًا ۚ أَفَطَالَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْعَهْدُ أَمْ أَرَدتُّمْ أَن يَحِلَّ عَلَيْكُمْ غَضَبٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُم مَّوْعِدِى
versets.tr: fa-rajaʿa mūsā ilā qawmihi ghaḍbāna asifan qāla yā qawmi alam yaʿidkum rabbukum waʿdan ḥasanan afa-ṭāla ʿalaykumu l-ʿahdu am aradtum an yaḥilla ʿalaykum ghaḍabun min rabbikum fa-akhlaftum mawʿidī
versets.trad: So Moses returned to his people, angry and grieved. He said: “O my people, did your Lord not make you a fair promise? Did the covenant seem too long to you, or did you wish that wrath from your Lord should descend upon you, so that you broke your promise to me?”
versets.commentaire: Moses returns angry and grieved. Qushayrī contrasts two returns: our Prophet returned from the Ascension filled with expansion, bringing his companions the gift of prayer and the honor of intimate nearness; Moses returned to his people adorned with anger and sorrow, and addressed them with reproach. How different the two! They had thought ill of their prophet regarding his promise, and this baneful suspicion made them deviate from the pact. So it is with whoever does not keep his covenant: he is threaded into this chain.

mots.racine: غ ض ب / أ س ف
ط و ل / ع ه د
mots.sens: did the covenant seem long to you

mots.racine: خ ل ف / و ع د

versets.ar: قَالُوا۟ مَآ أَخْلَفْنَا مَوْعِدَكَ بِمَلْكِنَا وَلَٰكِنَّا حُمِّلْنَآ أَوْزَارًا مِّن زِينَةِ ٱلْقَوْمِ فَقَذَفْنَٰهَا فَكَذَٰلِكَ أَلْقَى ٱلسَّامِرِىُّ
versets.tr: qālū mā akhlafnā mawʿidaka bi-malkinā wa-lākinnā ḥummilnā awzāran min zīnati l-qawmi fa-qadhafnāhā fa-kadhālika alqā s-sāmiriyyu
versets.trad: They said: “We did not break our promise to you of our own will; but we were laden with burdens of the people's ornaments, so we cast them [into the fire], and thus the Sāmirī threw.”
versets.commentaire: They plead ignorance of the consequences. Qushayrī: they said they had not, at first, intended what came about, nor known where their state would lead; the Copts' ornaments they had been made to carry, the Sāmirī forged the calf from them. Thus the unlawful, taken from the spoils of this world, is not without the baneful effect of its trace. Those ornaments borrowed from the Copts were unlawful for them, and they were the cause of their worship of the calf. Likewise, whoever plunges into the quest of the world by unlawful ways puts his religion in peril.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / م ل ك
mots.sens: we did not fail of our own will

mots.racine: و ز ر / ز ي ن

versets.ar: فَأَخْرَجَ لَهُمْ عِجْلًا جَسَدًا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٌ فَقَالُوا۟ هَٰذَآ إِلَٰهُكُمْ وَإِلَٰهُ مُوسَىٰ فَنَسِىَ
versets.tr: fa-akhraja lahum ʿijlan jasadan lahu khuwārun fa-qālū hādhā ilāhukum wa-ilāhu mūsā fa-nasiya
versets.trad: Then he brought forth for them a calf, a body that lowed. And they said: “This is your god and the god of Moses, but he has forgotten!”
versets.commentaire: The calf appears, and the latent idolatry bursts forth. Qushayrī: it is said that, having passed a people worshiping idols, they had asked Moses for a god like theirs; this inclination to worship the calf lay hidden, concealed in their hearts, and the Sāmirī gave it form. There is a sign here: when the hidden folds of passion lie in the heart, until that idolatry is rooted out by the chisel of spiritual struggle, one fears for the one who bears it. Forty days sufficed to lead them astray.

mots.racine: ع ج ل / ج س د / خ و ر
mots.sens: a calf, a body that lowed

mots.racine: أ ل ه

versets.ar: أَفَلَا يَرَوْنَ أَلَّا يَرْجِعُ إِلَيْهِمْ قَوْلًا وَلَا يَمْلِكُ لَهُمْ ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا
versets.tr: afa-lā yarawna allā yarjiʿu ilayhim qawlan wa-lā yamliku lahum ḍarran wa-lā nafʿan
versets.trad: Did they not see that it returned to them no word, and possessed for them neither harm nor benefit?
versets.commentaire: The mute idol neither answers nor profits. Qushayrī: God shows that what has no speech does not speak, and what holds neither harm nor benefit does not deserve worship. There is here a refutation of whoever does not acknowledge in the Real, from all eternity, Speech, nor describes Him by power over good and evil. The idol is the exact inverse of the living God.

mots.racine: ر ج ع / ق و ل
mots.sens: it returns to them no word

mots.racine: م ل ك / ض ر ر / ن ف ع
mots.sens: it possesses neither harm nor benefit

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ قَالَ لَهُمْ هَٰرُونُ مِن قَبْلُ يَٰقَوْمِ إِنَّمَا فُتِنتُم بِهِۦ ۖ وَإِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنُ فَٱتَّبِعُونِى وَأَطِيعُوٓا۟ أَمْرِى
versets.tr: wa-laqad qāla lahum hārūnu min qablu yā qawmi innamā futintum bihi wa-inna rabbakumu r-raḥmānu fa-ttabiʿūnī wa-aṭīʿū amrī
versets.trad: Aaron had already said to them: “O my people! You are only being tried by it. Your Lord is truly the All-Merciful. So follow me and obey my command!”
versets.commentaire: Aaron had warned them, in vain. Qushayrī: they did not observe Moses' command, who was above Aaron; now whoever does not respect the command of one higher in rank, how would he respect the command of one lower than himself? Whoever abandons the Real's command, how can one hope that he honor the masters and the people? Hence it has been said: there is no sanctity for the corrupt, for whoever abandons God's right, when would he keep the right of creatures?

mots.racine: ف ت ن
mots.sens: you are only tried by it

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ر ح م

versets.ar: قَالُوا۟ لَن نَّبْرَحَ عَلَيْهِ عَٰكِفِينَ حَتَّىٰ يَرْجِعَ إِلَيْنَا مُوسَىٰ
versets.tr: qālū lan nabraḥa ʿalayhi ʿākifīna ḥattā yarjiʿa ilaynā mūsā
versets.trad: They said: “We will not cease to be devoted to it until Moses returns to us.”
versets.commentaire: They persist under a pretext. Qushayrī: it was on their part an evasion; they claimed to be resolved to leave the worship of the calf once certain that Moses called them to pure tawḥīd. But every man of pretext leans on what he invokes as a vain argument. The false always finds an excuse to defer the truth it senses.

mots.racine: ب ر ح / ع ك ف
mots.sens: we will not cease being devoted to it

mots.racine: ر ج ع

versets.ar: قَالَ يَٰهَٰرُونُ مَا مَنَعَكَ إِذْ رَأَيْتَهُمْ ضَلُّوٓا۟
versets.tr: qāla yā hārūnu mā manaʿaka idh raʾaytahum ḍallū
versets.trad: Moses said: “O Aaron! What held you back, when you saw them going astray,
versets.commentaire: Moses questions Aaron vehemently. Qushayrī: Moses' heart contracted at seeing with his own eyes the worship of the calf; he had indeed heard from God that the Sāmirī had led them astray. But, as is said, the report is not like direct sight: when he saw the thing with his eyes, his heart contracted. And it has been said: when the heart contracts, the tongue widens. Moses' anger was but zeal for God.

mots.racine: م ن ع
ر أ ي / ض ل ل
mots.sens: when you saw them go astray

versets.ar: أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِ ۖ أَفَعَصَيْتَ أَمْرِى
versets.tr: allā tattabiʿani afa-ʿaṣayta amrī
versets.trad: from following me? Did you disobey my command?”
versets.commentaire: “Did you disobey my command?” Qushayrī: it is the contraction of Moses' heart pouring out in vehement speech toward his brother; but Aaron, in his forbearance and his knowledge — knowing that all this belonged to their Lord's decree — received each thing with contentment. Moses' reproach and Aaron's gentleness are two faces of one same attachment to God.

mots.racine: ت ب ع
ع ص ي / أ م ر

versets.ar: قَالَ يَبْنَؤُمَّ لَا تَأْخُذْ بِلِحْيَتِى وَلَا بِرَأْسِىٓ ۖ إِنِّى خَشِيتُ أَن تَقُولَ فَرَّقْتَ بَيْنَ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَلَمْ تَرْقُبْ قَوْلِى
versets.tr: qāla ya-bnaʾumma lā taʾkhudh bi-liḥyatī wa-lā bi-raʾsī innī khashītu an taqūla farraqta bayna banī isrāʾīla wa-lam tarqub qawlī
versets.trad: He replied: “O son of my mother! Take me neither by my beard nor by my head. I truly feared you would say: "You have caused division among the Children of Israel and did not heed my word."”
versets.commentaire: Aaron answers vehemence with gentleness. Qushayrī: when from Moses appeared what appeared, Aaron faced him with forbearance, delicacy, and good management — and such is the duty in companionship, that the matter not turn to estrangement. He coaxed him in his speech and sought his tenderness. Aaron could have reminded Moses that it was he who had asked for him as helper; but out of clemency and out of knowledge that all belonged to the divine decree, he met everything with contentment.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ل ح ي
mots.sens: take me not by my beard

mots.racine: خ ش ي / ف ر ق
mots.sens: I feared you would say: you divided

versets.ar: قَالَ فَمَا خَطْبُكَ يَٰسَٰمِرِىُّ
versets.tr: qāla fa-mā khaṭbuka yā sāmiriyyu
versets.trad: Moses said: “And what is your case, O Sāmirī?”
versets.commentaire: Moses turns to the Sāmirī. Qushayrī: Moses questioned each of them in his own way. And his rebuke of his people, his demand of reckoning from his brother, his inner turmoil and the grip of anger upon him — none of this changed the decree nor deferred what was judged. The agitation of man, even a prophet, does not move the measure fixed by God.

mots.racine: خ ط ب

versets.ar: قَالَ بَصُرْتُ بِمَا لَمْ يَبْصُرُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَقَبَضْتُ قَبْضَةً مِّنْ أَثَرِ ٱلرَّسُولِ فَنَبَذْتُهَا وَكَذَٰلِكَ سَوَّلَتْ لِى نَفْسِى
versets.tr: qāla baṣurtu bi-mā lam yabṣurū bihi fa-qabaḍtu qabḍatan min athari r-rasūli fa-nabadhtuhā wa-kadhālika sawwalat lī nafsī
versets.trad: He said: “I saw what they did not see. I took a handful from the track of the Messenger and cast it, for thus my soul prompted me.”
versets.commentaire: The Sāmirī invokes a vision and his soul's prompting. Qushayrī: “I knew what the Children of Israel did not know” — he claimed to have seen Gabriel and seized the dust beneath his mount's hoof, believing it the cause of the calf's life; “thus my soul prompted me,” and he followed its desire. Then came his ruin, that none might think himself safe from the hidden design of destiny, nor trust what appearance offers of gentleness — for perhaps, in truth, it is a ruse.

mots.racine: ب ص ر
mots.sens: I saw what they did not see

mots.racine: س و ل / ن ف س
mots.def: sawwala: to make seem fair, to entice.

versets.ar: قَالَ فَٱذْهَبْ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَ ۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوْعِدًا لَّن تُخْلَفَهُۥ ۖ وَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِى ظَلْتَ عَلَيْهِ عَاكِفًا ۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ نَسْفًا
versets.tr: qāla fa-dhhab fa-inna laka fī l-ḥayāti an taqūla lā misāsa wa-inna laka mawʿidan lan tukhlafahu wa-nẓur ilā ilāhika lladhī ẓalta ʿalayhi ʿākifan la-nuḥarriqannahu thumma la-nansifannahu fī l-yammi nasfan
versets.trad: He said: “Begone! In this life you shall say: "Touch me not!" And there is for you an appointment you will not fail. Look at your god, to which you have stayed devoted: We will surely burn it, then scatter its ashes in the sea.”
versets.commentaire: The calf will be burned and scattered. Qushayrī: all that the heart attaches itself to apart from God, the Real scatters — out of love for His beloved. That is why the idols will be cast tomorrow into the fire with the unbelievers, though they have neither crime, nor obligation, nor knowledge: they are but inert matter. Moses did not ignore that destiny acts and that creation belongs to God alone; yet he demanded the Sāmirī's punishment, so that it be known that if the decree comes from God, reproach and reckoning weigh upon the creature.

mots.racine: م س س
ح ر ق / ن س ف
mots.sens: We will burn it then scatter it
mots.def: ḥarraqa: to burn; nasafa: to scatter.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَآ إِلَٰهُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى لَآ إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ عِلْمًا
versets.tr: innamā ilāhukumu llāhu lladhī lā ilāha illā huwa wasiʿa kulla shayʾin ʿilman
versets.trad: Your only god is God, beside whom there is no god. He embraces all things in knowledge.
versets.commentaire: Oneness is reaffirmed against the idol. Qushayrī: your god, whose worship is incumbent upon you by the right of His command, is God, beside whom none is god — He robed in Majesty, from whom nothing of the knowable escapes. He is not like that calf, inert matter that knows not, can not, lives not, hears not nor sees — which He can crush and burn. The contrast between the Living who embraces all and the mute idol completes the demonstration.

mots.racine: أ ل ه
و س ع / ع ل م
mots.sens: He embraces all things in knowledge

versets.ar: كَذَٰلِكَ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ مَا قَدْ سَبَقَ ۚ وَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَٰكَ مِن لَّدُنَّا ذِكْرًا
versets.tr: kadhālika naquṣṣu ʿalayka min anbāʾi mā qad sabaqa wa-qad ātaynāka min ladunnā dhikran
versets.trad: Thus do We relate to you some of the tidings of what has gone before. And We have given you from Us a Reminder.
versets.commentaire: God entrusts to His Prophet the stories of the ancients. Qushayrī: We make known to you the states of the first and the last, that nothing of their ways remain obscure to you, that you adorn yourself with their virtues and gather in yourself their scattered merits. But know that We have raised none to your rank; We have given you from Us an honor that none shares, We have reminded you of the ancient pact knit with Us, and renewed before them Our election of you and the nobility of Our favor upon you.

mots.ar: نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ مَا قَدْ سَبَقَ
mots.tr: naquṣṣu ʿalayka min anbāʾi mā qad sabaqa
mots.racine: ق ص ص / ن ب أ
mots.sens: We relate to you tidings of the past

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ذ ك ر
mots.sens: We gave you from Us a Reminder
mots.def: min ladun: from the presence of.

versets.ar: مَّنْ أَعْرَضَ عَنْهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ يَحْمِلُ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَٰمَةِ وِزْرًا
versets.tr: man aʿraḍa ʿanhu fa-innahu yaḥmilu yawma l-qiyāmati wizran
versets.trad: Whoever turns away from it will bear on the Day of Resurrection a burden,
versets.commentaire: Whoever turns from the Reminder will bear a burden. Qushayrī: those who turn away from it through associationism will bear tomorrow a weight and a load; they have distanced themselves from the place of election and had no rank in Realization, so their punishment does not exceed the pains of their soul and the burning of their body. But as for the people of election, should they forget Him for an hour, neglect Him for an instant, at once there falls upon their head a trial before which, in Hell, every other punishment fades.

mots.racine: ع ر ض
ح م ل / ق و م / و ز ر
mots.sens: he will bear on the Day of Judgment a burden

versets.ar: خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهِ ۖ وَسَآءَ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَٰمَةِ حِمْلًا
versets.tr: khālidīna fīhi wa-sāʾa lahum yawma l-qiyāmati ḥimlan
versets.trad: abiding therein forever. Evil is the load for them on the Day of Resurrection,
versets.commentaire: The load remains forever. Qushayrī, in the spirit of the sūra: the eternity of the burden answers the obstinacy of the turning away; what one refused to lay down at God's feet during life becomes an immovable weight on the Day of Judgment. The permanence of the punishment is but the reflection of the perseverance in refusal.

mots.racine: خ ل د
س و أ / ح م ل
mots.sens: evil is the load for them

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ ۚ وَنَحْشُرُ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ زُرْقًا
versets.tr: yawma yunfakhu fī ṣ-ṣūri wa-naḥshuru l-mujrimīna yawmaʾidhin zurqan
versets.trad: the Day the trumpet is blown! That Day We shall gather the criminals, with eyes glazed.
versets.commentaire: At the trumpet's blast, the criminals are gathered.
