Qushayrī: some, on the Day of Resurrection, have a deferred term — after the blowing in the trumpet, according to what is reported in the Book and the tradition. But others have an anticipated resurrection, here below: a present reckoning, an actual demand, a punishment already come — for just as punishments fall in the hereafter upon the outward of some, punishments fall in this very life upon the secrets of others.

mots.racine: ن ف خ / ص و ر
ح ش ر / ز ر ق
mots.sens: We gather the criminals, blue-eyed, glazed

versets.ar: يَتَخَٰفَتُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ إِن لَّبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا عَشْرًا
versets.tr: yatakhāfatūna baynahum in labithtum illā ʿashran
versets.trad: They will whisper among themselves: “You stayed [in the grave] only ten [days]!”
versets.commentaire: They murmur over the brevity of their stay. Qushayrī: whoever busies himself counting times and distinguishing the diverse states is of a kind that does not exhaust its trial — his affair is light. But whoever's discourse aims at the very Meaning does not linger to describe his state: his states speak for him, and he is not questioned about the report. The counting of days is the concern of one not absorbed by the Essential.

mots.racine: خ ف ت
ل ب ث / ع ش ر

versets.ar: نَّحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ إِذْ يَقُولُ أَمْثَلُهُمْ طَرِيقَةً إِن لَّبِثْتُمْ إِلَّا يَوْمًا
versets.tr: naḥnu aʿlamu bimā yaqūlūna idh yaqūlu amthaluhum ṭarīqatan in labithtum illā yawman
versets.trad: We know best what they will say, when the most exemplary of them in conduct says: “You stayed only a day!”
versets.commentaire: The most discerning among them reduces the stay to a single day. Qushayrī, in the spirit of the sūra: the finer the gaze, the more the duration of this world contracts; on the scale of eternity, a whole life is but a day. The most “upright of way” is the one who best measures the vanity of the elapsed delay.

mots.racine: م ث ل / ط ر ق
mots.def: amthal: the most upright; ṭarīqa: conduct.

mots.racine: ل ب ث / ي و م

versets.ar: وَيَسْـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلْجِبَالِ فَقُلْ يَنسِفُهَا رَبِّى نَسْفًا
versets.tr: wa-yasʾalūnaka ʿani l-jibāli fa-qul yansifuhā rabbī nasfan
versets.trad: They ask you about the mountains. Say: “My Lord will scatter them as dust,
versets.commentaire: The very mountains will be reduced to dust. Qushayrī: just as in the promised Resurrection the mountains change their state and become like carded wool, so it is in the Resurrection already present in hearts: of it none inform you but the very great, firm as summits; states seize them that efface them to their own eyes and ravish them from their peers — such is God's law.

mots.racine: س أ ل / ج ب ل
mots.sens: they ask you about the mountains

mots.racine: ن س ف

versets.ar: فَيَذَرُهَا قَاعًا صَفْصَفًا
versets.tr: fa-yadharuhā qāʿan ṣafṣafan
versets.trad: and leave it a level plain,
versets.commentaire: The earth becomes a bare, smooth plain. Qushayrī, in the register of the sūra: what was relief is effaced, what was height is leveled; and likewise, in the experience of the close ones, the soul's roughnesses are razed until there remains only the bare expanse of pure servitude.

mots.racine: ق و ع / ص ف ف

versets.ar: لَّا تَرَىٰ فِيهَا عِوَجًا وَلَآ أَمْتًا
versets.tr: lā tarā fīhā ʿiwajan wa-lā amtan
versets.trad: in which you will see neither crookedness nor elevation.”
versets.commentaire: No curve, no elevation remains. Qushayrī, in the spirit of the sūra: the effacement of all relief figures the effacement of all claim; where there is no longer hollow nor rise, there is no longer room for the “I” — only the perfect evenness before the Real's majesty remains.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ع و ج
mots.sens: you see in it no crookedness

mots.racine: أ م ت

versets.ar: يَوْمَئِذٍ يَتَّبِعُونَ ٱلدَّاعِىَ لَا عِوَجَ لَهُۥ ۖ وَخَشَعَتِ ٱلْأَصْوَاتُ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ فَلَا تَسْمَعُ إِلَّا هَمْسًا
versets.tr: yawmaʾidhin yattabiʿūna d-dāʿiya lā ʿiwaja lahu wa-khashaʿati l-aṣwātu li-r-raḥmāni fa-lā tasmaʿu illā hamsan
versets.trad: That Day they will follow the Caller in whom there is no crookedness. Voices will be hushed before the All-Merciful, and you will hear nothing but a whisper.
versets.commentaire: Before the All-Merciful, voices fall silent and only a murmur remains. Qushayrī: imaginations break, understandings halt, reasons withdraw, sciences are effaced, knowledges are confounded; all that is an attribute of the creature vanishes, and the reign of Reality prevails. Then there is neither trace nor vestige, neither mark nor ruin: in the Presence, muteness; on the carpet, annihilation; for attributes, effacement — and true soundness is in fixity.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / د ع و
mots.sens: they follow the Caller, no crookedness in him

mots.racine: خ ش ع / ص و ت / ه م س
mots.def: khashaʿa: to be humbled; hams: whisper.

versets.ar: يَوْمَئِذٍ لَّا تَنفَعُ ٱلشَّفَٰعَةُ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ ٱلرَّحْمَٰنُ وَرَضِىَ لَهُۥ قَوْلًا
versets.tr: yawmaʾidhin lā tanfaʿu sh-shafāʿatu illā man adhina lahu r-raḥmānu wa-raḍiya lahu qawlan
versets.trad: That Day intercession will avail none, save him whom the All-Merciful permits and whose word He approves.
versets.commentaire: Intercession avails only by divine permission. Qushayrī: the implied sense is that he for whom intercession is permitted, his intercession profits; and if the intercession of anyone is approved by the All-Merciful's permission, it is impossible that the Messenger's not be approved, he the most excellent of all. The intercession of the great among His elect is accepted for the most humble, in the deferred as in the immediate; and the Real makes the masters intercede this very day for their disciples.

mots.racine: ن ف ع / ش ف ع
أ ذ ن / ر ح م

versets.ar: يَعْلَمُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمَا خَلْفَهُمْ وَلَا يُحِيطُونَ بِهِۦ عِلْمًا
versets.tr: yaʿlamu mā bayna aydīhim wa-mā khalfahum wa-lā yuḥīṭūna bihi ʿilman
versets.trad: He knows what is before them and what is behind them, while they encompass Him not in knowledge.
versets.commentaire: His knowledge embraces all; theirs does not embrace Him. Qushayrī: nothing of their past or their future escapes the Real, but they do not enclose Him in knowledge. The pronoun “Him” may refer to what is before and behind them, or to the Real Himself — such is the way of the ancients: they say that God knows the creature, but that knowledge does not encompass Him, just as they say: He is seen but not grasped.

mots.racine: ي د ي / خ ل ف
mots.sens: what is before and behind them

mots.racine: ح و ط / ع ل م
mots.sens: they encompass Him not in knowledge

versets.ar: وَعَنَتِ ٱلْوُجُوهُ لِلْحَىِّ ٱلْقَيُّومِ ۖ وَقَدْ خَابَ مَنْ حَمَلَ ظُلْمًا
versets.tr: wa-ʿanati l-wujūhu li-l-ḥayyi l-qayyūmi wa-qad khāba man ḥamala ẓulman
versets.trad: Faces will be humbled before the Living, the Self-Subsisting. And whoever bears iniquity has failed,
versets.commentaire: Faces bow before the Living, the Immutable. Qushayrī: necks bend, creation submits to His judgment, tyrants are humbled before Him; and whoever has committed iniquity remains in his darkness — according to the measure of his fault, more or less. Before the Ḥayy Qayyūm, all haughtiness collapses.

mots.racine: ع ن و / ح ي ي / ق و م
mots.sens: faces are humbled before the Living Subsisting
mots.def: ʿanā: to submit; al-Ḥayy al-Qayyūm: divine Names.

mots.racine: خ ي ب / ح م ل / ظ ل م
mots.sens: he has failed who bore iniquity

versets.ar: وَمَن يَعْمَلْ مِنَ ٱلصَّٰلِحَٰتِ وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَلَا يَخَافُ ظُلْمًا وَلَا هَضْمًا
versets.tr: wa-man yaʿmal mina ṣ-ṣāliḥāti wa-huwa muʾminun fa-lā yakhāfu ẓulman wa-lā haḍman
versets.trad: while whoever does righteous deeds, being a believer, will fear neither injustice nor diminution.
versets.commentaire: The believer who works fears neither injustice nor loss. Qushayrī: the righteous deed is that fit for acceptance, and its author is one stripped of the blemishes that obstruct the reality of the act; it is also said: the righteous deed is that whose author has not hastened the reward. And “being a believer” means: believing that his Lord gives the believer nothing by virtue of his faith, but by His sole grace — faith being its sign, not its compelling cause.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح / أ م ن
mots.sens: does righteous deeds while a believer

mots.racine: خ و ف / ظ ل م / ه ض م
mots.sens: he fears neither injustice nor diminution

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلْنَٰهُ قُرْءَانًا عَرَبِيًّا وَصَرَّفْنَا فِيهِ مِنَ ٱلْوَعِيدِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ أَوْ يُحْدِثُ لَهُمْ ذِكْرًا
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika anzalnāhu qurʾānan ʿarabiyyan wa-ṣarrafnā fīhi mina l-waʿīdi laʿallahum yattaqūna aw yuḥdithu lahum dhikran
versets.trad: Thus We have sent it down as an Arabic Qurʾān, and We have varied therein the warnings, that perhaps they may fear God, or that it may stir in them a remembrance.
versets.commentaire: The Arabic Qurʾān unfolds the warnings to awaken fear. Qushayrī: We followed proof upon proof, messenger after messenger, and warned them by manifold ways of teaching and the manifestation of many signs. The diversity of the warnings (taṣrīf) answers the diversity of hearts: to each the way by which the reminder can reach him.

mots.racine: ق ر أ / ع ر ب
ص ر ف / و ع د

versets.ar: فَتَعَٰلَى ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَلِكُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۗ وَلَا تَعْجَلْ بِٱلْقُرْءَانِ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يُقْضَىٰٓ إِلَيْكَ وَحْيُهُۥ ۖ وَقُل رَّبِّ زِدْنِى عِلْمًا
versets.tr: fa-taʿālā llāhu l-maliku l-ḥaqqu wa-lā taʿjal bi-l-qurʾāni min qabli an yuqḍā ilayka waḥyuhu wa-qul rabbi zidnī ʿilman
versets.trad: Exalted is God, the King, the Truth! Do not hasten with the Qurʾān before its revelation to you is completed, but say: “My Lord, increase me in knowledge!”
versets.commentaire: Summit of the sūra: “My Lord, increase me in knowledge!” Qushayrī: if it is said to the most learned of men, the lord of Arabs and non-Arabs, to him whose privileges of knowledge the Real attests — “say: Lord, increase me in knowledge” — it is because he knows that what the Real grants His close ones of the subtleties of knowledge is without limit. The Prophet asks for his Lord's knowledge directly, without intermediary; when he says “I am the most knowing of you in God and the most fearing,” it is answered to him: “say: Lord, increase me in knowledge” — that it be known that the noblest quality of the servant is to stand in the place of need, adorned with the attribute of asking and not of claiming.

mots.racine: ع ل و / م ل ك / ح ق ق
mots.sens: exalted is God, the King, the Truth
mots.def: al-Malik: the King; al-Ḥaqq: the Truth, the Existent.

mots.racine: ز ي د / ع ل م
mots.sens: say: Lord, increase me in knowledge

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ عَهِدْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ ءَادَمَ مِن قَبْلُ فَنَسِىَ وَلَمْ نَجِدْ لَهُۥ عَزْمًا
versets.tr: wa-laqad ʿahidnā ilā ādama min qablu fa-nasiya wa-lam najid lahu ʿazman
versets.trad: And We had made a covenant with Adam before, but he forgot, and We found in him no firm resolve.
versets.commentaire: Adam forgot the covenant. Qushayrī: We found in him neither perfected strength nor firmness in observing the command, until the mark of disobedience was set upon him. It is also said: We found in him no resolve to persist in opposition — and if there was fault, it was through the effect of forgetting. And it is said: the account of Adam's fault was set forth to his descendants to soothe their hearts, that they not despair of God's mercy — for Adam himself bore this mark, he the first of men.

mots.racine: ع ه د
mots.sens: We made a covenant with Adam

mots.racine: ن س ي / ع ز م
mots.sens: he forgot, with no firm resolve

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَٰٓئِكَةِ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِءَادَمَ فَسَجَدُوٓا۟ إِلَّآ إِبْلِيسَ أَبَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-idh qulnā li-l-malāʾikati sjudū li-ādama fa-sajadū illā iblīsa abā
versets.trad: When We said to the angels: “Prostrate yourselves before Adam,” they prostrated, except Iblīs, who refused.
versets.commentaire: The angels prostrate, Iblīs refuses. Qushayrī: prostration is a form of humility and magnification; no obedience nor worship of Adam had preceded it — the Real created him with His hand, raised his rank after instructing him, carried him to the Garden, and commanded the angels of every heaven to prostrate before him, to honor him and to test them. All prostrated; only Iblīs held back and received the humiliation inscribed for him in the decree.

mots.racine: س ج د
أ ب ي

versets.ar: فَقُلْنَا يَٰٓـَٔادَمُ إِنَّ هَٰذَا عَدُوٌّ لَّكَ وَلِزَوْجِكَ فَلَا يُخْرِجَنَّكُمَا مِنَ ٱلْجَنَّةِ فَتَشْقَىٰٓ
versets.tr: fa-qulnā yā ādamu inna hādhā ʿaduwwun laka wa-li-zawjika fa-lā yukhrijannakumā mina l-jannati fa-tashqā
versets.trad: So We said: “O Adam, this is an enemy to you and to your wife. Let him not drive you both out of the Garden, lest you be wretched.
versets.commentaire: Adam is warned of Iblīs's enmity. Qushayrī: the counsel did not avail them, for He had willed for them what He warned them of, and knew they would meet what He frightened them with. As for attributing wretchedness to Adam alone — though both bear the world's toil — it is by accord with the verse-endings, or because labor weighs on the man more than on the woman. Whoever lends his ear to the word of his enemy swallows remorse, and it no longer avails him.

mots.racine: ع د و / ز و ج
mots.sens: an enemy to you and your wife

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ش ق و
mots.sens: let him not expel you, lest you toil

versets.ar: إِنَّ لَكَ أَلَّا تَجُوعَ فِيهَا وَلَا تَعْرَىٰ
versets.tr: inna laka allā tajūʿa fīhā wa-lā taʿrā
versets.trad: Indeed, in it you will neither hunger nor be naked;
versets.commentaire: In the Garden, neither hunger nor nakedness. Qushayrī: no assent more complete than Adam's, no counsel more imbued with mercy than God's, no certainty stronger than his — but Adam had not yet tasted wretchedness; when the trial came and he knew the toil and labor of which he had been warned, he repented and wept long, but after the decree was sealed.

mots.racine: ج و ع
ع ر ي

versets.ar: وَأَنَّكَ لَا تَظْمَؤُا۟ فِيهَا وَلَا تَضْحَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-annaka lā taẓmaʾu fīhā wa-lā taḍḥā
versets.trad: and that you will neither thirst therein nor suffer the sun's heat.”
versets.commentaire: Neither thirst nor the sun's heat in the Garden. Qushayrī: Adam enjoyed the Garden without knowing its worth, until here below hunger, thirst, and trial in all their forms had seized him. And each time a new trial befell him, he would weep; Gabriel would come and say: “Your Lord greets you and asks: why do you weep?” And he would remind Gabriel: was this not what was said, “you will neither thirst nor suffer the sun”? One knows the worth of grace only at its loss.

mots.racine: ظ م أ
ض ح و
mots.def: ḍaḥiya: to be exposed to the sun.

versets.ar: فَوَسْوَسَ إِلَيْهِ ٱلشَّيْطَٰنُ قَالَ يَٰٓـَٔادَمُ هَلْ أَدُلُّكَ عَلَىٰ شَجَرَةِ ٱلْخُلْدِ وَمُلْكٍ لَّا يَبْلَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-waswasa ilayhi sh-shayṭānu qāla yā ādamu hal adulluka ʿalā shajarati l-khuldi wa-mulkin lā yablā
versets.trad: But Satan whispered to him, saying: “O Adam! Shall I show you the tree of immortality and a kingdom that never decays?”
versets.commentaire: Satan tempts Adam by the desire for immortality. Qushayrī: the devil tempted him, and the Real knew it; Adam did not then remember that this came from the insinuations of the one of whom it had been said “he is an enemy to you.” And it is said: when Adam coveted everlasting permanence, the devil found through his temptation a path to him. The true breach was the desire to endure forever — it was by this that the enemy slipped in.

mots.racine: و س و س / ش ط ن
ش ج ر / خ ل د / م ل ك
mots.sens: the tree of eternity and a kingdom that decays not
mots.def: khuld: immortality; balā: to wear out.

versets.ar: فَأَكَلَا مِنْهَا فَبَدَتْ لَهُمَا سَوْءَٰتُهُمَا وَطَفِقَا يَخْصِفَانِ عَلَيْهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۚ وَعَصَىٰٓ ءَادَمُ رَبَّهُۥ فَغَوَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-akalā minhā fa-badat lahumā sawʾātuhumā wa-ṭafiqā yakhṣifāni ʿalayhimā min waraqi l-jannati wa-ʿaṣā ādamu rabbahu fa-ghawā
versets.trad: So they both ate of it, and their nakedness became apparent to them, and they began to stitch over themselves leaves of the Garden. And Adam disobeyed his Lord and erred.
versets.commentaire: The fault consummated, their nakedness appears. Qushayrī: when they committed the forbidden, there appeared what one is ashamed of; but God was gentle with them in saying “their nakedness became apparent to them” — not “appeared” absolutely — that is, none but they saw it. It is also said: when they stripped off the garment of piety, their outward garment was scattered. And “Adam disobeyed”: that it be known the gravity of faults lies in the transgression of the Commander and in His grandeur, not in their number.

mots.racine: ب د و / س و أ
mots.sens: their nakedness became apparent to them
mots.def: badā: to appear; sawʾāt: private parts.

mots.racine: ع ص ي / غ و ي
mots.sens: Adam disobeyed his Lord and erred

versets.ar: ثُمَّ ٱجْتَبَٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَتَابَ عَلَيْهِ وَهَدَىٰ
versets.tr: thumma jtabāhu rabbuhu fa-tāba ʿalayhi wa-hadā
versets.trad: Then his Lord chose him, accepted his repentance, and guided him.
versets.commentaire: After the fault comes election. Qushayrī: He announces that after his disobedience, and after all he had done, his Lord chose him — He who had first chosen him with no cause whatsoever chose him again after the fall, accepted his repentance, and forgave his sin. “And He guided him,” that is: He guided him toward Himself, until he made his excuse and begged forgiveness. The second election, after the fault, is sweeter still than the first.

mots.racine: ج ب ي
ت و ب / ه د ي
mots.sens: He accepted his repentance and guided

versets.ar: قَالَ ٱهْبِطَا مِنْهَا جَمِيعًۢا ۖ بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ ۖ فَإِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُم مِّنِّى هُدًى فَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ هُدَاىَ فَلَا يَضِلُّ وَلَا يَشْقَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla hbiṭā minhā jamīʿan baʿḍukum li-baʿḍin ʿaduwwun fa-immā yaʾtiyannakum minnī hudan fa-mani ttabaʿa hudāya fa-lā yaḍillu wa-lā yashqā
versets.trad: He said: “Go down from it, both of you together, enemies one to another. Then if guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows My guidance will not stray nor be wretched.
versets.commentaire: The descent comes with a promise: guidance. Qushayrī: He set enmity between Adam, Iblīs, and the serpent; trials followed one upon another for Adam and Eve after their leaving the Garden — the mark of disobedience, separation from the Garden, entry into the world, the devil's hostility, the trial of desires. Then He said: “whoever follows My guidance” and leaves his passion, acting not by the enemy's insinuation — to him all good, and no harm touches him.

mots.racine: ه ب ط / ج م ع
mots.sens: go down from it both together

mots.ar: فَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ هُدَاىَ فَلَا يَضِلُّ وَلَا يَشْقَىٰ
mots.tr: fa-mani ttabaʿa hudāya fa-lā yaḍillu wa-lā yashqā
mots.racine: ت ب ع / ه د ي / ض ل ل / ش ق و
mots.sens: whoever follows My guidance strays not nor toils

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَعْرَضَ عَن ذِكْرِى فَإِنَّ لَهُۥ مَعِيشَةً ضَنكًا وَنَحْشُرُهُۥ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَٰمَةِ أَعْمَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-man aʿraḍa ʿan dhikrī fa-inna lahu maʿīshatan ḍankan wa-naḥshuruhu yawma l-qiyāmati aʿmā
versets.trad: But whoever turns away from My remembrance will have a straitened life, and We will raise him blind on the Day of Resurrection.”
versets.commentaire: To turn from the Reminder is a life in constraint. Qushayrī: whoever turns from engaging in the ways of concord, upon him surge the forms of abandonment; whoever turns from persevering in His remembrance by the heart, upon him accumulates the heart's dispersion that strips him of all rest; whoever turns from the intimacy of His remembrance, upon him open the devil's insinuations and the soul's cares, which earn him inner desolation and the closing of the doors of ease and expansion.

mots.racine: ع ر ض / ذ ك ر
ع ي ش / ض ن ك

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ لِمَ حَشَرْتَنِىٓ أَعْمَىٰ وَقَدْ كُنتُ بَصِيرًا
versets.tr: qāla rabbi lima ḥashartanī aʿmā wa-qad kuntu baṣīran
versets.trad: He will say: “My Lord! Why have You raised me blind, when I was sighted?”
versets.commentaire: The blind of the hereafter protests his past sight. Qushayrī: according to the tradition, “each is raised in the state in which he died”; whoever was blind of heart in this world is gathered in his state, whoever lived in ignorance is gathered upon ignorance. The blindness of the Judgment is but the prolongation of the heart's blindness here below.

mots.racine: ح ش ر / ع م ي
mots.sens: why have You raised me blind

mots.racine: ب ص ر

versets.ar: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ أَتَتْكَ ءَايَٰتُنَا فَنَسِيتَهَا ۖ وَكَذَٰلِكَ ٱلْيَوْمَ تُنسَىٰ
versets.tr: qāla kadhālika atatka āyātunā fa-nasītahā wa-kadhālika l-yawma tunsā
versets.trad: He will say: “Thus it is: Our signs came to you and you forgot them; and thus today you are forgotten.”
versets.commentaire: Forgetting answers forgetting. Qushayrī: just as they neglect today the meditation of His signs, so will they be neglected tomorrow in the punishment, with no mercy for the weakness of their states. Whoever forgets God's signs will be forgotten in turn — not that God forgets, but that He withholds His attention from the one who has turned away.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ن س ي
mots.sens: Our signs came to you and you forgot them

mots.racine: ن س ي

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى مَنْ أَسْرَفَ وَلَمْ يُؤْمِنۢ بِـَٔايَٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ ۚ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ أَشَدُّ وَأَبْقَىٰٓ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika najzī man asrafa wa-lam yuʾmin bi-āyāti rabbihi wa-la-ʿadhābu l-ākhirati ashaddu wa-abqā
versets.trad: Thus do We requite whoever transgresses and does not believe in the signs of his Lord. And the punishment of the hereafter is harsher and more lasting.
versets.commentaire: Requital follows the measure of the act. Qushayrī: His law has established that He requites each according to what befits his state; what the servant has advanced for himself, he will reap its consequences — good for good, evil for evil. The punishment of the hereafter surpasses in severity and duration every pain of this world.

mots.racine: ج ز ي / س ر ف
ع ذ ب / ش د د / ب ق ي
mots.sens: the punishment of the hereafter, harsher and lasting

versets.ar: أَفَلَمْ يَهْدِ لَهُمْ كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْقُرُونِ يَمْشُونَ فِى مَسَٰكِنِهِمْ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَءَايَٰتٍ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلنُّهَىٰ
versets.tr: afa-lam yahdi lahum kam ahlaknā qablahum mina l-qurūni yamshūna fī masākinihim inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-ulī n-nuhā
versets.trad: Has it not guided them, how many generations We destroyed before them, amid whose dwellings they now walk? In that are signs for those endowed with understanding.
versets.commentaire: The ruins of past generations are a teaching. Qushayrī: do they not look so as to reflect? And if they see clearly, do they not take the lesson? And if they ponder, do they not refrain from evil? Or do they run, faces turned, in the fields of their heedlessness, without returning from their evil deeds? To walk among the vestiges of the destroyed and learn nothing is the height of blindness.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ن
mots.sens: how many generations We destroyed before them

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ن ه ي
mots.sens: signs for those endowed with understanding

versets.ar: وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَكَانَ لِزَامًا وَأَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى
versets.tr: wa-lawlā kalimatun sabaqat min rabbika la-kāna lizāman wa-ajalun musamman
versets.trad: And were it not for a word that preceded from your Lord, [the punishment] would have been inevitable, and a fixed term [is set].
versets.commentaire: An earlier decree defers the punishment. Qushayrī: were it not for God's word already pronounced to defer the penalty of this community, and that He will not exterminate it — for a host of His close ones are in the loins of these people — He would have hastened their punishment; but He has granted them a known delay, without yet abandoning them. The reprieve is not forgetfulness: it shelters the elect-to-come hidden in the descendants of the present.

mots.racine: ك ل م / س ب ق
mots.sens: a word that preceded from your Lord

mots.racine: أ ج ل / س م و

versets.ar: فَٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ ٱلشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ غُرُوبِهَا ۖ وَمِنْ ءَانَآئِ ٱلَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْ وَأَطْرَافَ ٱلنَّهَارِ لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضَىٰ
versets.tr: fa-ṣbir ʿalā mā yaqūlūna wa-sabbiḥ bi-ḥamdi rabbika qabla ṭulūʿi sh-shamsi wa-qabla ghurūbihā wa-min ānāʾi l-layli fa-sabbiḥ wa-aṭrāfa n-nahāri laʿallaka tarḍā
versets.trad: So endure patiently what they say, and glorify the praise of your Lord before the rising of the sun and before its setting; and during the hours of the night glorify, and at the ends of the day, that perhaps you may be content.
versets.commentaire: To the trial of words, the remedy is glorification. Qushayrī: to hear offense imposes pain; He therefore removed from His Prophet the pain that seized him at these words, commanding him: if hearing what they say grieves you, Our glorification — by which you praise Us — relaxes you. “Before the rising of the sun”: at the day's beginning, that it be blessed and your morning sweet; “before its setting”: at the day's decline, that your night be good; “during the hours of the night”: for the perfection of the elect is in remembering God in solitude; “at the ends of the day”: maintain the remembrance of God in all your states.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ق و ل
س ب ح / ح م د
mots.sens: glorify the praise of your Lord

mots.racine: ر ض و

versets.ar: وَلَا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيْنَيْكَ إِلَىٰ مَا مَتَّعْنَا بِهِۦٓ أَزْوَٰجًا مِّنْهُمْ زَهْرَةَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا لِنَفْتِنَهُمْ فِيهِ ۚ وَرِزْقُ رَبِّكَ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-lā tamuddanna ʿaynayka ilā mā mattaʿnā bihi azwājan minhum zahrata l-ḥayāti d-dunyā li-naftinahum fīhi wa-rizqu rabbika khayrun wa-abqā
versets.trad: And do not stretch your eyes toward what We have given certain couples among them to enjoy — the splendor of the life of this world — that We may try them by it. The provision of your Lord is better and more lasting.
versets.commentaire: Let the gaze not stretch toward the world's splendor. Qushayrī: the Real has, over all His beloved's states, a jealousy: He does not consent that he expend a single one of his motions, his rests, his states in what holds no pleasure for God. When He had trained him not to look upon the world's adornment with the fullness of his gaze, he stood upon the earth on one foot, out of reserve, until it was said to him “Ṭā Hā,” that is: tread the earth with both your feet. And “the provision of your Lord is better and more lasting”: the little of the lawful, where the All-Merciful's pleasure resides, is better than the abundance of unlawful laden with His wrath; a little that makes you contemplate your Lord is better than much that makes you forget Him.

mots.racine: م د د / ع ي ن
ز ه ر / ح ي ي / د ن و
mots.sens: the splendor of the life of this world

mots.racine: ر ز ق / خ ي ر / ب ق ي
mots.sens: your Lord's provision is better and more lasting

versets.ar: وَأْمُرْ أَهْلَكَ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱصْطَبِرْ عَلَيْهَا ۖ لَا نَسْـَٔلُكَ رِزْقًا ۖ نَّحْنُ نَرْزُقُكَ ۗ وَٱلْعَٰقِبَةُ لِلتَّقْوَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-ʾmur ahlaka bi-ṣ-ṣalāti wa-ṣṭabir ʿalayhā lā nasʾaluka rizqan naḥnu narzuquka wa-l-ʿāqibatu li-t-taqwā
versets.trad: And enjoin prayer upon your family, and be steadfast in it. We ask of you no provision; it is We who provide for you. And the [good] outcome is for piety.
versets.commentaire: Prayer opens the door of provision. Qushayrī: prayer is the opening of the door of rizq, and upon it He relies for facilitating the gifts at the time of need. It is also said: prayer is the provision of hearts, and their healing is in it; when the soul's food is delayed, the heart's food is strengthened. And “We ask of you no provision, it is We who provide for you”: whoever contemplates and is realized in the “We” is delivered from every distinction between one provision and another; He has lightened for the poor the trial of scarcity and the delay of provision by this one word: “We.”

mots.racine: أ م ر / ص ل و
ر ز ق
mots.sens: it is We who provide for you

mots.racine: ع ق ب / و ق ي

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا۟ لَوْلَا يَأْتِينَا بِـَٔايَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦٓ ۚ أَوَلَمْ تَأْتِهِم بَيِّنَةُ مَا فِى ٱلصُّحُفِ ٱلْأُولَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-qālū lawlā yaʾtīnā bi-āyatin min rabbihi awa-lam taʾtihim bayyinatu mā fī ṣ-ṣuḥufi l-ūlā
versets.trad: They said: “Why does he not bring us a sign from his Lord?” Has the clear proof of what is in the former scriptures not come to them?
versets.commentaire: They demand a sign though the proof has already come. Qushayrī: their inner sights have gone blind; they claim no proof accompanies him — but the defect was not in the proofs, it was in their inner vision. Were God to gather for them every sign demanded of a messenger, and did He not will that they believe, they would only increase in excess, unbelief, and ruin. Such is the custom of their forefathers in denying the prophets.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / أ ي ي
mots.sens: why does he not bring us a sign

mots.racine: ب ي ن / ص ح ف
mots.sens: the proof of what is in the former scriptures

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّآ أَهْلَكْنَٰهُم بِعَذَابٍ مِّن قَبْلِهِۦ لَقَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا لَوْلَآ أَرْسَلْتَ إِلَيْنَا رَسُولًا فَنَتَّبِعَ ءَايَٰتِكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن نَّذِلَّ وَنَخْزَىٰ
versets.tr: wa-law annā ahlaknāhum bi-ʿadhābin min qablihi la-qālū rabbanā lawlā arsalta ilaynā rasūlan fa-nattabiʿa āyātika min qabli an nadhilla wa-nakhzā
versets.trad: And had We destroyed them with a punishment before it (the coming of Muhammad), they would have said: “Our Lord! Why did You not send us a messenger, that we might follow Your signs before being humbled and disgraced?”
versets.commentaire: Without a messenger, they would have pleaded the excuse. Qushayrī: if We send them messengers, they meet them with a thousand denials; and if We deprived them of one to treat them as they deserve, they would say: why did You not dispatch a messenger that we might believe? Their chains do not break, and their states do not loose from what displeases. Such is the way of one who leans neither toward union nor toward love: he always invents a pretext for the rupture.

mots.racine: ر س ل
mots.sens: why did You not send us a messenger

mots.racine: ذ ل ل / خ ز ي
mots.def: dhalla: to be abased; khaziya: to be disgraced.

versets.ar: قُلْ كُلٌّ مُّتَرَبِّصٌ فَتَرَبَّصُوا۟ ۖ فَسَتَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ أَصْحَٰبُ ٱلصِّرَٰطِ ٱلسَّوِىِّ وَمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ
versets.tr: qul kullun mutarabbiṣun fa-tarabbaṣū fa-sa-taʿlamūna man aṣḥābu ṣ-ṣirāṭi s-sawiyyi wa-mani htadā
versets.trad: Say: “Each is waiting, so wait. You will soon know who are the people of the even path and who is rightly guided!”
versets.commentaire: The sūra is sealed on shared waiting. Qushayrī: all stand in conjecture, without assured certitude, awaiting what will appear in the future; but the people of dispersion await what the spheres, the natures, and the stars will impose, as they believe, while the believers await what will unveil of the divine decrees — they in the breath of tawḥīd, the others in the darkness of associationism. The believer's waiting is repose in the One; the denier's, anguish in the many.

mots.racine: ر ب ص
ص ح ب / ص ر ط / س و ي / ه د ي
mots.sens: the people of the even path and who is guided
mots.def: ṣirāṭ sawiyy: the even, straight path.
